data.summary: fable-audit epic (260701) — vibe-cognition
data.detail: Parent epic for all findings from the 2026-07-01 Fable-coordinated fact-finding audit. Confirmed intended purpose (the audit yardstick): Vibe Cognition is a fully local MCP server plugin for Claude Code that gives a codebase persistent, structured memory — a git-committed knowledge graph (.cognition/journal.jsonl) of decisions, failures, discoveries, constraints, incidents, patterns, workflows, and open tasks — so future Claude Code sessions (and human teammates) understand why the code is the way it is, without re-litigating settled choices or repeating known failures. Primary users are developers using Claude Code daily, and multi-agent / multi-human team collaboration is a first-class use case, not an edge case. Local embeddings (no API keys) power semantic search; the browser dashboard is a secondary, nice-to-have surface — the graph, MCP tools, hooks, and skills are the primary surfaces. Curation is agent-driven via /vibe-curate by current design (it could be automated again in the future, but that is not the current design). Success for a new consumer: install from the marketplace, restart Claude Code, and within a session or two see Claude spontaneously recording history, retrieving relevant context at session start, and — after /vibe-curate — having a browsable, linked graph. Stage docs: docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md, docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md, docs/260701-fable-audit-consumer.md.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Fable Audit Stage 1 — Per-System Audit (2026-07-01)
data.detail:
# Fable Audit — Stage 1: Per-System Audit — 2026-07-01

Confirmed intended purpose: Vibe Cognition is a fully local MCP server plugin for Claude Code that gives a codebase persistent, structured memory — a git-committed knowledge graph (.cognition/journal.jsonl) of decisions, failures, discoveries, constraints, incidents, patterns, workflows, and open tasks. Multi-agent / multi-human team collaboration is first-class. Local embeddings power semantic search; dashboard is secondary. Curation is agent-driven via /vibe-curate by current design. Success: install from marketplace, restart, and within a session or two see Claude recording history, retrieving context at session start, and after /vibe-curate having a browsable linked graph.

Scope: seven parallel Sonnet auditors, one per system: core graph & persistence; documents/tasks/workflows; embeddings & search; MCP server & tools; session lifecycle & install/upgrade; skills layer; dashboard (weighted secondary). 39 raw findings synthesized.

FINDINGS (condensed; full detail in docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md):

1. [critical/blindspot] Journal rehydrate-reset is completely silent — _catch_up() detects a shrunk/replaced journal and _rehydrate_reset() discards all in-memory state with only logger.info; no tool response, get_status field, or prime flag ever reports nodes lost. Incident 5d63a548783d was real; mitigation was process-only (constraint 1f39e60c6d83). storage.py:829-930.
2. [high/gap] Byte-rewrite defense (-text gitattribute) withheld from consumer teams — git_hygiene/readme teach only merge=union; C-3 replay defense survives via autocrlf coincidence. Deliberate decision 9f13a8099e03 but residual risk never disclosed. git_hygiene.py:39, readme.py:83-103.
3. [high/gap] Home ChromaDB collection has no model/dim drift guard — model_guard only on foreign attach (XP1); home drift → vector_search broad except → [] → search silently empty. Zero tests cover home drift or Ollama backend. storage.py:181-190, cognition
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Surface journal rehydrate-reset events (loss visibility)
data.detail: _catch_up()/_rehydrate_reset() silently discards in-memory state on journal shrink/replace (storage.py:829-930, info-only log). Add: WARNING log with node-count delta, a get_status field (e.g. last_rehydrate + nodes_discarded), and a flag in the next prime injection so a session learns memory was lost. Incident 5d63a548783d is the precedent; current mitigation is process-rules only. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Fix broken getting-started cognition_record example in readme.py
data.detail: COGNITION_GETTING_STARTED (readme.py:120-121) shows cognition_record(type=...) — wrong param name (should be node_type) and omits required context/author. A newcomer following the canonical onboarding literally fails on their first write. Fix the example to match the real signature (cognition_tools.py:1439-1448); add a drift test comparing example params to the live signature. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Home-collection embedding model/dim drift guard + get_status surfacing
data.detail: The XP1 model_guard protects only cognition_load_project; the home project's own ChromaDB collection is never checked, so config-vs-stored model/dim drift makes vector_search raise → broad except → [] → cognition_search returns a successful-looking empty result (embeddings/storage.py:181-190; guard at cognition_tools.py:1296-1356). Apply the guard to the home path, surface drift in get_status, and add a regression test (currently zero tests cover home drift — test_xp1_registry.py only tests foreign attach). From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Validate node_type in cognition_search via _parse_node_type
data.detail: cognition_search passes node_type unvalidated into the Chroma where filter (cognition_tools.py:426-451 home; :1934-1973 multi-project) — a typo like "descision" returns {"results":[],"count":0} indistinguishable from no history. Every other type-filterable read tool validates via _parse_node_type and errors clearly. WP-T T-6 (episode 49ef4310ba17) deliberately held scope and search was never revisited. Wire _parse_node_type into both search paths + test. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Consumer journal byte-rewrite defense: teach/offer -text or disclose residual risk
data.detail: git_hygiene.py (:39) and readme.py team setup (:83-103) teach only merge=union, never -text; the C-3 byte-offset replay defense survives checkout/merge only via the autocrlf=true/CRLF-write coincidence (git_hygiene.py:8-16). The dev repo protects itself with -text (.gitattributes:8-24) via a cut-over ritual consumers never see. Decision 9f13a8099e03 deliberately withheld -text; the missing piece is either a safe self-service cutover for consumer teams or honest user-facing disclosure of the residual risk. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Re-embed on journal replay (close structural node↔embedding drift)
data.detail: _replay_entry (storage.py:976-1041) never touches embeddings, so nodes journaled by another process are visible to get_history but invisible to cognition_search until server restart. Discovery 4b99fa9f44d5 called the drift structural; v0.13.0 (ef95333d105c) removed only the post-commit-hook source — "broader replay/reload drift persists" per its own edge reason. Embed on replay (or add a periodic reconcile) so teammate nodes become searchable live. Coordinate with E-8 batching task (be019b3eea3c). From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Amend WP-Server-Lifecycle harm register: orphaned dashboard = live DELETE listener
data.detail: docs/wp-server-lifecycle-plan.md §1 frames orphan harm as RAM/handles/update-corruption only. An orphan that launched the dashboard also keeps a live, token-gated, DELETE-capable HTTP listener on 127.0.0.1 for the orphan's lifetime (dashboard/server.py:169-224, daemon thread with no liveness tie-in; orphans observed up to 3 days). Add this angle to the plan before dispatch of task a54b0191e362. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Dashboard token hygiene: stop INFO-logging URL+token; document transcript exposure
data.detail: The full-capability dashboard token (gates read/download/DELETE) is returned verbatim in the MCP tool result (dashboard_tool.py:42) — persisted in the conversation transcript — and logger.info'd with the URL (dashboard/server.py:114,198). Network-layer hardening never modeled this out-of-band leak. Stop logging the token at INFO; consider short-lived/rotating tokens; document the transcript exposure. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Delete provenance: actor in tombstone, docstring gaps, delete auth-rejection test
data.detail: remove_node journals {"action":"remove_node","data":{"id":...}} with no author (storage.py:283-287) — the dashboard's confirm()-gated click (app.js:226-245) deletes with zero record of who/why. Also: cognition_remove_node docstring omits the unlinked_artifacts key it returns for documents (operations.py:113-123) and never warns that deleting a parent task detaches children (F10 behavior, stale parent_id reported live); DELETE /api/node is the only gated endpoint with no auth-rejection test (test_dashboard.py:227-238). Add actor to the tombstone, fix the docstring, add the test. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Graceful server lifespan startup failure handling
data.detail: In lifespan() only Settings() is wrapped (log-and-reraise); CognitionStorage and ChromaDBStorage init (server.py:244-267) are unguarded — corrupted Chroma sqlite or .cognition/ permission errors kill the server with a raw traceback Claude Code may never surface, leaving zero cognition tools and no explanation. Catch init failures, log diagnosably (file breadcrumb), and either degrade gracefully or exit with a clear one-line cause. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Re-run tool-surface self-sufficiency audit repo-wide + drift tests
data.detail: Docstring/return-shape drift found in multiple tools: get_status promises embedding_model/embedding_ready keys that don't exist and returns undocumented embedding_status/curation (service_tools.py:21-92); cognition_store_document's references param silently redirects to context, untaught by /vibe-document (cognition_tools.py:1717-1718, deliberate at storage layer per e752ff313ad7 but unexplained at surface). The recurring workflow 67751ebc39bd exists but was last scoped to newly-shipped tools only. Re-run against ALL tools and skills; add cheap drift regression tests. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: migrate_mcp: guard the write path like the read path
data.detail: remove_server_entry guards json.load but neither write branch guards _atomic_write (migrate_mcp.py:95-100,110-114) — PermissionError/OSError (editor/AV lock on Windows) escapes as an unhandled traceback; the hook swallows it (session-start.sh:98-100) so the stale .mcp.json entry silently survives, outranking the plugin-declared server (decision a906f12a6ef7). Wrap writes, return a structured failure note the hook can surface. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: First-install robustness: hook timeout sizing + multi-cause health-probe message
data.detail: SessionStart hook timeout is 120s (hooks.json:6-13) while a cold uv sync downloads torch/chromadb/sentence-transformers — a timeout kill mid-sync lands users in the half-installed state. The health-probe failure message (session-start.sh:70-85) unconditionally diagnoses the DLL-lock class ("close ALL sessions") though the probe fires identically for interrupted downloads/disk-full/timeout kills. Size the timeout against a measured cold sync (or make sync resumable/backgrounded) and make the message enumerate causes. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Shell-script test coverage for session-start.sh / reinject-instructions.sh
data.detail: Neither hook script has any automated test (path normalization, cygpath branching, hash-stamp gating, ${var%[/\\]*} expansion, JSON assembly/'{}' fallback) — and this exact class already shipped a regression (B-3 backslash bug, discovery 41e24b74219d), fixed by hand-verification. Add a bash-level regression harness (bats or pytest+subprocess on Git Bash) covering the branchy logic on both path styles. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Validate task priority against documented vocabulary
data.detail: cognition_add_task/_update_task document priority as critical|high|normal|low but write any string into severity unvalidated (cognition_tools.py:901-918, 1214-1216) — unlike status, validated one function away. Typos silently sort into the "normal" band (SEVERITY_ORDER fallback) and evade cognition_list_tasks(priority=...) filters. Mirror the status validation pattern; add a test for invalid priority. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Document staleness: proactive surfacing + supersedes on re-store
data.detail: Drift is detected only on explicit cognition_get_document (cognition_tools.py:729-742); search/startup-sync/prime never re-check, so a changed source file serves stale embedded content through cognition_search indefinitely. Re-storing a changed file mints a new unlinked node with no supersedes edge (manual-only by design, :2229-2234). Passive pull-detection was deliberate (e752ff313ad7 §8d); add: staleness flags in search results/get_status, and auto-offer (or auto-create) a supersedes link when re-storing a path whose prior doc node exists. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Fix /vibe-curate skill drift (source kwarg, part_of claim, concurrency, warm-up)
data.detail: Four spec defects: (a) SKILL.md:45 instructs a top-level source kwarg on cognition_add_edges_batch that doesn't exist — source is a per-edge JSON key defaulting to "batch", and edge-analyzer's output schema omits it, so curate provenance silently degrades; (b) SKILL.md:62 / edge-analyzer.md:40 claim agent part_of is "already forbidden" — nothing in code enforces it (only duplicate_of blocked), and the skill itself creates part_of in step 3.4; Haiku subagents (pinned per e983273d722e) are most likely to trust the claim — fix the wording or enforce in code; (c) add a coordination note for concurrent /vibe-curate runs (stateless get_uncurated_nodes, no claim mechanism — duplicate LLM work); (d) document the loading_embeddings warm-up failure in the embedding-dependent skills (currently only vibe-dashboard mentions it). From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Widen cluster-analyzer scan beyond get_history(limit=50)
data.detail: cluster-analyzer.md:7 uses cognition_get_history(limit=50) as its sole candidate source — no pagination or context sweep — so once a graph exceeds 50 nodes, older regions are permanently invisible to clustering (this project is at 281 nodes). Couple it to the uncurated/edgeless worklists or add a paged/context-term sweep. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Wire snapshot_journal into the manager-flush procedure
data.detail: journal_io.snapshot_journal (journal_io.py:146-169) was built (CR-4) so manager flushes never capture a torn mid-append tail — but it has zero production callers; the real worktree-flush protocol is a manual copy. Both recorded incidents in this area (5d63a548783d, 59416463f1e3) are where a torn-tail-safe copy matters. Expose it (console script or documented one-liner) and reference it in the flush constraint/workflow docs. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Re-open backfill skill/CLI drift (S-3 sub-item closed by coarse resolved_by)
data.detail: Two disagreeing backfill mechanisms persist: /vibe-backfill skill (watermark-based, documented) vs vibe-cognition-backfill CLI (hardcoded 30-day window, no --days, undocumented — README mentions neither it nor vibe-cognition-prime). The June audit's S-3 flagged this; task f70c9ad1c55d was closed by a coarse resolved_by to the v0.12.2 episode though the shipped scope (aace1420ce90) covered only S-3(b)/(e). Reconcile the two mechanisms (retire or document the CLI, align window logic; fold in H-6(b) --days), and note the meta-lesson: enumerate shipped-vs-dropped sub-items when closing multi-item tasks. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Ollama backend: nomic query/document prefix parity + first tests
data.detail: OllamaBackend.encode accepts is_query but ignores it (generator.py:100-118) while the ST backend's search_query:/search_document: prefixes are load-bearing for nomic retrieval quality (:37-38,71-72) — same nomic model family by default. Apply the prefixes (or record a deliberate decision not to), and add the first OllamaBackend tests (currently zero). From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Reorder _materialize_blob: gitignore entry before writing local_only blob
data.detail: write_blob (cognition_tools.py:480) runs before add_gitignore_entry (:494) on the local_only path — a brief window where a privacy-intended blob sits unignored and a coincident git add/commit could publish it. Reorder the two calls. From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Decide fate of redirect_edges/duplicate_of dead code; optional workflow-supersedes guardrails
data.detail: CognitionStorage.redirect_edges (storage.py:339-391) has no production caller and doesn't remove old edges (would double edges if resumed as-is); both edge tools refuse duplicate_of ("requires merge logic... not supported") — a modeled-then-shelved merge feature with no recorded decision. Implement the merge feature or remove the dead code. Optionally: guardrails on workflow supersession (type restriction, cycle prevention — get_workflow_head is cycle-safe but returns an implementation-defined HEAD on cycles, queries.py:114-150). From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Fable Audit Stage 2 — Interconnectivity Audit (2026-07-01)
data.detail:
# Fable Audit — Stage 2: Interconnectivity — 2026-07-01

Seam-based audit (five parallel Sonnet auditors split by boundary, not system): write-path data flow, process topology, env/config contract, context-injection pipeline, git↔journal↔graph. Yardstick: the confirmed intent (multi-agent first-class, dashboard secondary, agent-driven curation). Full detail in docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md.

FINDINGS (condensed):

1. [high/bug] ChromaDB double-recreate race: __init__ never stamps embed_scheme (only recreate_collection does), and each server's background thread checks a process-local startup-frozen metadata snapshot — so EVERY fresh install triggers the migration, and two same-project sessions in the 2-30s model-load window both call recreate_collection(); the second delete-recreates after the first synced, silently wiping its vectors (broad except swallows it). git_hygiene solves the identical one-time-migration problem with a file lock — inconsistent discipline. embeddings/storage.py:43-77, server.py:184-235.
2. [high/broken-assumption] Compact drops the entire prime digest: compact hook reinjects only static SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS; constraints/open-tasks/patterns/decisions/incidents are never restored and no cue says they're stale — defeats "the graph IS the backlog" in exactly the long sessions it serves. Reinject decision 7e9665028bb1 was scoped to instructions-survival only; data payload never weighed. hooks.json:5-24, instructions.py:50-63, prime.py:170-186.
3. [high/bug] Merge-shaped journal replay silently drops edges/removals: _replay_entry drops add_edge/remove_edge/remove_node with missing endpoints with NO log and the offset advances forever; merge=union (the supported separate-clones mechanism) can interleave lines edge-before-node. No merge-shaped replay tests exist. Maintainers use shared-checkout so this topology is never exercised. storage.py:1001-1029, 900-919.
4. [high/bug] Startup embedding reconciler is a drifted second implementatio
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Stamp embed_scheme at collection creation + file-lock the recreate migration
data.detail: ChromaDBStorage.__init__ never stamps embed_scheme (only recreate_collection does, embeddings/storage.py:43-77) and each server's background thread checks a process-local startup-frozen metadata snapshot (server.py:219-225) — so every fresh install triggers the migration and two same-project sessions in the model-load window can double-delete-recreate the collection, the second silently wiping the first's synced vectors. Fix: stamp at creation (legacy-only trigger), re-query live metadata, and guard the recreate with a file lock (mirror git_hygiene._acquire_lock, git_hygiene.py:63-90). Add a two-process repro test (partially satisfies T-1c c34c788b8d5b). From fable-audit Stage 2 (docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Compact hook: restore prime digest (or inject staleness cue)
data.detail: On compact, reinject-instructions.sh restores only static SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS; the prime data digest (constraints, open tasks, patterns, decisions, incidents) is silently lost with no cue to re-fetch — defeating "the graph IS the backlog" (SKILL.md:62-68) in exactly the long sessions that compact. Decision 7e9665028bb1 scoped reinject to instructions-survival only; the data payload was never weighed. Fix: have the compact hook also run a trimmed generate_prime(), or at minimum append "task/constraint view is stale — re-fetch via cognition_list_tasks". hooks/hooks.json:5-24, instructions.py:50-63, prime.py:170-186. From fable-audit Stage 2 (docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Merge-shaped replay defense: log/retry dropped actions + merge-shaped tests
data.detail: _replay_entry silently drops add_edge/remove_edge/remove_node whose endpoints aren't yet in the graph (storage.py:1001-1029 — no log branch; offset advances forever, :900-919). merge=union — the supported separate-clones mechanism — can interleave divergent tails so an edge line precedes its endpoint's node line, permanently and invisibly losing edges after an ordinary git merge. No test constructs a merge-shaped journal. Fix: log every dropped action at WARNING, add a deferred-retry pass for out-of-order lines within one catch-up, and add merge-shaped journal tests (edge-before-node, duplicate add_node, edge to unmerged node). Maintainers' shared-checkout workflow never exercises this topology (4ed473ba9c75) — this closes the gap or justifies de-scoping separate-clones support. From fable-audit Stage 2 (docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Route startup embedding sync through the shared embed paths (kill the drifted inline copy)
data.detail: _sync_cognition_embeddings (server.py:108-124) reimplements the entity embed inline — omitting task status/owner from embed text/metadata and routing workflow nodes through the generic branch (one untruncated vector, no _embed_workflow chunking) — violating the "single shared embed path" invariant (pattern c6f92230831c, _embed_entity_node at cognition_tools.py:58-98, _embed_workflow :101-136). Nodes created in the 2-30s model-load window (e.g. _add_task defers, :981) get permanently degraded vectors; workflows have no repair path (in-place edit refused :791-800). Fix: call the canonical functions from the reconciler; add a reconciler==writer parity test. From fable-audit Stage 2 (docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Harden REPO_PATH: env_ignore_empty + validation + unify env-read patterns
data.detail: plugin.json sets REPO_PATH=${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR} unconditionally; if substitution yields an empty string, pydantic-settings (no env_ignore_empty, config.py:26-30) treats present-but-empty as explicit, skipping the fallback factory — Path("") = cwd = the plugin install root (uv run --directory), so .cognition/ is silently created in the plugin checkout. Empirically verified. config.py/prime.py:204/backfill.py:81 are all vulnerable; only dashboard/cli.py:89's `or` guard is safe — the inconsistency proves it was never a decision. Fix: env_ignore_empty=True, validate repo_path non-empty and plausible, unify the three read patterns; add the empty-CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR case to the human install-gate checklist (constraint b8ec24fe9107). From fable-audit Stage 2 (docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Episode dedup contract for shared commit refs + make duplicate_of reachable
data.detail: Two clones/agents independently mint distinct episodes for the same commit:<sha> — _record_node does no find_nodes_by_ref lookup (cognition_tools.py:159-178; contrast documents' sha-dedup, storage.py:122-142); both survive merges forever, and the repair edge duplicate_of is refused by the edge tools AND forbidden by /vibe-curate's instructions, with redirect_edges unreachable. Fix: ref-lookup before mint (warn or reuse), or merge-time duplicate detection; implement the duplicate_of merge flow or amend the curate-skill ban so curators can at least mark duplicates. Upgrades the motivation of task 4800d5d16adb (redirect_edges dead-code decision). From fable-audit Stage 2 (docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Deterministic-edge sweep: re-evaluate against reference index, not has-any-edge
data.detail: _create_deterministic_edges_for_edgeless (server.py:22-49) skips any node with ANY edge, so a node linked locally is never reconsidered for the part_of edge to a teammate's later-merged episode sharing the same commit: ref — cross-clone linkage stays permanently partial, and get_edgeless_nodes (cognition_tools.py:2299-2309) shares the coarse predicate so it can't surface the gap. Fix: predicate = "missing deterministic edges its references warrant" (diff expected-vs-present against the reference index). From fable-audit Stage 2 (docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Chunk completeness: verify full chunk set in the reconciler, not chunk-0
data.detail: _embed_document/_embed_workflow are delete-then-write loops; the startup reconciler's completeness probe is only "<id>#chunk-0 present" (server.py:104-106) — a crash mid-loop leaves chunks 1..N permanently missing while reading as fully synced. Fix: record expected chunk count in the node-level Chroma metadata at write time and verify the full set during reconcile. From fable-audit Stage 2 (docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Pin Settings.env_file to repo_path/.env or remove it
data.detail: Settings uses env_file=".env" (config.py:23-30) resolved against process cwd — which is the SHARED plugin root for every project's server (uv run --directory, plugin.json:11-23). A project-level .env is inert; a plugin-root .env silently becomes global config for every project (embedding model, prime knobs). Pin env_file to the resolved repo_path, or drop it and document env-var-only config. From fable-audit Stage 2 (docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: CI check: pyproject.toml version == plugin.json version
data.detail: The release procedure requires bumping both files by hand; nothing in .github/workflows/ci.yml asserts they match (uv sync --locked only catches pyproject-vs-uv.lock drift, a37a60664cfc). A forgotten bump ships a plugin whose displayed version misrepresents the pinned code. Add a trivial CI step (or test) comparing the two version strings. From fable-audit Stage 2 (docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Journal-first ordering for document artifacts (or orphan-artifact reconciler)
data.detail: _store_document writes the text sidecar (:626) and blob (:633-642) BEFORE minting/journaling the node (:682), inverting the C-4 journal-first discipline — a crash in between orphans artifacts no reclaim path can find (delete-reclaim walks down from a node; no scanner checks .cognition/documents//sidecars for ownerless files; store_copy leaks into the git-committed tree). Either reorder (journal a pending marker first) or add an orphan-artifact scan to startup reconcile. From fable-audit Stage 2 (docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Consolidate onboarding source of truth; count SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS in token accounting
data.detail: The record→curate loop is restated in three independently-maintained channels (SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS instructions.py:19-43; ONBOARDING_BLOCK readme.py:133-146; COGNITION_GUIDE/GETTING_STARTED readme.py:7-130) — no contradiction yet but the same shape that produced the Stage-1 skill drift, concentrated at the first session. Also, the v0.13.0 "~53% cut" (CHANGELOG, ef95333d105c) is scoped to generate_prime() only and excludes the ~200-300-token SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS paid every session AND every compact. Pick one owning channel with the others referencing it; include instructions.py in any future session-start token accounting. From fable-audit Stage 2 (docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Fable Audit Stage 3 — Consumer Audit (2026-07-01)
data.detail:
# Fable Audit — Stage 3: Consumer Audit — 2026-07-01

Four Sonnet auditors as newcomers: literal README walkthrough; agent for whom tool docstrings ARE the docs; team adoption arcs (teammate #2, topology, attribution, cross-project); skills/CLI/error UX + payoff reachability. Yardstick: confirmed intent (install → within a session or two: recording, retrieval, and after /vibe-curate a browsable linked graph; multi-agent/multi-human first-class). Full detail in docs/260701-fable-audit-consumer.md.

FINDINGS (condensed):

1. [high/blindspot] Joining teammate told "ready" while search silently incomplete: embedding_ready.set() fires (server.py:206) BEFORE the backfill sync loop starts (:215); no "syncing" status exists in get_status (ready/loading/error only); README's only guidance covers model load, not corpus sync; the cognition_reload row's "auto-converges with concurrent sessions" overstates against high-severity drift discoveries (4b99fa9f44d5, 55b6740e42f8); no teammate-join walkthrough or timing expectations anywhere (onboarding fires only for EMPTY graphs).
2. [high/gap] Shared-checkout protocol unshipped: the maintainers' own topology (worktree flush, no journal commits on WP branches, destructive-op ban — constraints 1f39e60c6d83/4ed473ba9c75, born of incidents 5d63a548783d/59416463f1e3) exists only in the private graph + personal team-rules files; README mentions "shared-checkout" once undefined; cognition_readme teaches separate-clones only. Neither topology is both exercised and shipped (Stage 2 showed the shipped one is untested).
3. [high/bug+gap] README accuracy: Troubleshooting's "Only one Vibe Cognition instance can run per project" is stale-false (initial-commit vintage; contradicts rust-lock/multi-client discoveries and the maintainers' daily mode — real caveat is startup-hydration divergence); tool tables list 25 of 29 (missing cognition_readme + all three cross-project tools); cross-project feature has zero README presence; troubleshooting omits
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Teammate-join visibility: "syncing" status + join walkthrough + fix auto-converge claim
data.detail: embedding_ready.set() fires (server.py:206) BEFORE the backfill sync loop starts (:215), so a teammate joining an existing graph sees embedding_status "ready" while historical nodes are still un-embedded and search is silently incomplete; no "syncing" state exists (service_tools.py:21-82). Add a third status (ready only after sync, or expose sync progress/counts), surface in get_status, add a README "joining an existing graph" walkthrough with timing expectations, and correct the cognition_reload row's "auto-converges with concurrent sessions" overstatement (contradicts 4b99fa9f44d5/55b6740e42f8). Distinct from the E-8 perf task (be019b3eea3c) — this is the visibility gap. From fable-audit Stage 3 (docs/260701-fable-audit-consumer.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Ship the topology guide: shared-checkout protocol + how to choose
data.detail: The maintainers' shared-checkout discipline (no journal commits on WP branches, manager worktree-flush at checkpoints, hard destructive-op ban — constraints 1f39e60c6d83/4ed473ba9c75, born of real incidents 5d63a548783d/59416463f1e3) exists only in the private graph and personal team-rules files. README mentions "shared-checkout" once, undefined (README.md:223); cognition_readme teaches separate-clones/merge=union only (readme.py:83-103). Write a user-facing topology guide: the two modes, how to pick, and the full shared-checkout protocol. Pairs with the Stage-2 merge-shaped-replay task (d6cd1495b23a): today neither topology is both exercised and shipped. From fable-audit Stage 3 (docs/260701-fable-audit-consumer.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: README accuracy pass: stale concurrency claim, 4 missing tools, cross-project section, troubleshooting, first-run expectations
data.detail: Fix in one pass: (a) replace "Only one Vibe Cognition instance can run per project at a time" (README.md:305, initial-commit vintage, contradicts rust-lock/multi-client discoveries and the maintainers' daily multi-agent mode) with the real caveat (per-process startup hydration divergence, 55b6740e42f8); (b) add the 4 missing tools to the tables (cognition_readme, cognition_load_project, cognition_unload_project, cognition_list_projects — tables list 25 of the advertised 29); (c) add a cross-project section (shipped v0.9.0, zero README presence); (d) troubleshooting entries for the venv self-heal class ("close ALL sessions", hooks/session-start.sh:60-85, incident 3432e00e483d) and Windows orphan pileup (a54b0191e362); (e) Quick Start: mention the uv-sync + model-download two-stage wait up front and add an install-success signal / "how to verify it's working" paragraph; (f) add EMBEDDING_REVISION to the config table (config.py:71-77). From fable-audit Stage 3 (docs/260701-fable-audit-consumer.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Add edge-semantics table to cognition_add_edge/add_edges_batch docstrings
data.detail: The when-to-use/direction semantics for led_to/supersedes/contradicts/relates_to/resolved_by/part_of exist only in skills/vibe-curate/edge-analyzer.md:20-36 (loaded solely by the curate subagent), while cognition_add_edge's docstring gives a bare enum (cognition_tools.py:2216-2268) and cognition_record's docstring explicitly invites direct hand-linking (:1459). Port the semantics table into both edge tools' docstrings so the direct path stops being guesswork. From fable-audit Stage 3 (docs/260701-fable-audit-consumer.md).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Fix cognition_record docstring: scope deterministic-linking claim + surface document/task boundaries
data.detail: (a) "Always include references... so nodes link to their episode via deterministic part_of matching" (cognition_tools.py:1489-1494) reads general, but the truth table (storage.py:662-708) never links entity↔entity — mid-episode decision/discovery clusters citing the same issue sit fully edgeless until curation. Scope the claim to the actual table and state that entity↔entity linking requires curation. Also, /vibe-cognition mandates auto-curate "without being asked" (SKILL.md:191-208) which fans out Haiku subagents per 5-10-node batch with no cost/scope warning; cadence guidance ("high number", "several") is unquantified. README never mentions author/attribution at all. Either server-resolve entity authorship like tasks (possibly keeping a separate free-text "persona" field), or document the asymmetry prominently (README + record docstring) so multi-human graph readers know which provenance is verified. data.detail:
# Fable-audit backlog burn-down — overnight autonomous run (2026-07-02)

Manager: Vince. Implementers: general-purpose subagents (one per WP), editing the single
shared checkout on `main` directly (human's explicit instruction). Implementers never
commit; Vince gates each diff (pytest w/ hard timeout, ruff, pyright-no-new-errors,
tautology + known-intentional checks) and commits+pushes per WP. Peer-reviewed by a
Sonnet subagent before execution; amendments folded in below.
[Execution note: implementation is dispatched to Vorpid (subordinate teammate), per Colton's instruction after plan commit.]

Scope: the 39 children of fable-audit epic `74740daa02fa` (docs/260701-fable-audit-*.md).
No release tonight: single version bump 0.13.0 → 0.14.0 at the end; human releases.
Tasks needing a product decision I can't reconcile from the cognition graph are skipped
with the open question appended to the task node.

## Standing acceptance criteria (every WP)
- `uv run pytest` green under a hard wall-clock timeout; `uv run ruff check .` clean;
  `uv run pyright` no NEW errors (1 pre-existing dashboard/server.py error is known).
- New tests are non-tautological (assert the specific failure reason; fail on revert).
- No new test may spawn real subprocesses/sockets/network (mock them); Windows handle
  cleanup in fixtures (Chroma clients, lock files).
- Any WP touching `get_status` updates its docstring in the same diff; docstring diffed
  against the actual return dict at the gate (WPs 1, 2, 4).
- Diff cross-checked against the known-intentional list before commit.

## Known-intentional (must NOT be "fixed")
Task-delete child-orphaning storage behavior (F10, tested); document drift pull-only
detection; merge=union-only auto-config (decision 9f13a8099e03 — the -text item is
disclosure only); cognition_record rejects node_type=task; workflow in-place-edit
refusal (supersession only); node-id mint TOCTOU accepted residual; Haiku pin for
curate subagents; dashboard localhost-only desig

Peer review (Sonnet) amendments folded: (1) -text byte-rewrite defense is DISCLOSURE ONLY — decision 9f13a8099e03 (merge=union-only auto-config) stands; (2) redirect_edges/duplicate_of fate is a product call → skip-with-question on 4800d5d16adb, WP-5 ships only dedup-contract + replay logging; (3) 5340ae677931's "ready only after backfill" option rejected — embedding_ready.set() timing unchanged (E-8 deferral deliberate), third "syncing" status value only, code half folded into WP-4 next to WP-3's edits of the same server.py chain; (4) two-process ChromaDB repro softened to same-process two-instance lock test reusing git_hygiene's lock primitive (Windows wedge risk), true multi-process variant skip-by-default. Also: pytest-timeout as WP-0 setup; compact hook pinned to re-running generate_prime() (already the trimmed digest) over inventing a new cue format. data.summary: Manager ran a revert-based tautology check by swapping storage.py IN the live shared tree — implementer's file-watcher caught the intermediate state (HEAD content + PS5.1 BOM) as a scare; isolated worktree was the rule
data.detail: During the WP-1 gate, Vince verified test non-tautology by overwriting src/vibe_cognition/cognition/storage.py with the HEAD version in the shared checkout (PowerShell 5.1 Set-Content -Encoding utf8 also injected a BOM), running the new tests, then restoring from a scratchpad backup. Final state was byte-correct and the suite passed, but Vorpid's editor/watcher fired mid-swap and showed a full revert + stray BOM — Vorpid correctly stopped everything and re-verified before proceeding. No data lost, but this is exactly why rules-managers.md says run gate verification at the pinned commit in an ISOLATED throwaway worktree, not the live tree two agents share. Lesson: revert checks and any file-swapping verification go in a temp git worktree (or wait until after the WP commit and revert against the committed SHA in isolation); PS5.1 Set-Content utf8 writes a BOM — use -Encoding utf8NoBOM semantics (or git checkout) when byte fidelity matters. Appended to the management ledger for ratification.

data.summary: Manager's worktree-targeted `git checkout <sha> -- src/` ran in the shared MAIN tree (shell cwd reset between tool calls) and clobbered implementer's uncommitted WP-3 edits; tests survived, editor-buffer restore recovered src
data.detail: During WP-2 gate verification in a throwaway worktree, two traps compounded. (1) Bare `uv run pytest` in the fresh worktree resolved a pytest whose imports came from the MAIN checkout's package — revert checks silently "passed" against live code; only `uv run python -m pytest` + an import-provenance check (`python -c "import vibe_cognition; print(__file__)"`) actually exercised the worktree. (2) The PowerShell working directory did NOT persist across tool calls (documented behavior says it does): a follow-up `git checkout 9283df8 -- src/` meant to restore the worktree executed in the shared main tree, overwriting Vorpid's uncommitted WP-3 edits to embeddings/storage.py and server.py. Survived: tests/test_e3_doc_prefix.py (different directory), cognition_tools.py, all of .cognition/ (journal safe). Recovery: Vorpid's editor buffers + their detailed progress report (which doubled as a re-implementation spec) restored storage.py within minutes. Mitigations now standing: every git command carries explicit `git -C <absolute-path>`; destructive ops re-verify with `rev-parse --show-toplevel` in the same command; worktree tests run via `uv run python -m pytest` after provenance check. This is the second shared-tree near-miss tonight (first: live-tree file swap during WP-1 gate) — same root class: manager verification mechanics touching the tree an implementer is live in.

data.detail:
CognitionStorage.redirect_edges (storage.py:339-391) has no production caller and doesn't remove old edges (would double edges if resumed as-is); both edge tools refuse duplicate_of ("requires merge logic... not supported") — a modeled-then-shelved merge feature with no recorded decision. Implement the merge feature or remove the dead code. Optionally: guardrails on workflow supersession (type restriction, cycle prevention — get_workflow_head is cycle-safe but returns an implementation-defined HEAD on cycles, queries.py:114-150). From fable-audit Stage 1 (docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md).

--- OPEN QUESTION FOR COLTON (parked by Vince, overnight fable burn-down 2026-07-02; peer review flagged this as a product call an autonomous run must not make) ---
Should duplicate_of/redirect_edges become a real, reachable merge feature, or be deleted as dead code?
Context gathered from the graph + tonight's work: (a) WP-5 (b36e4a79113a) shipped the DETECTION half — cognition_record now warns with possible_duplicate_of when a new episode shares a commit ref with an existing episode, but there is still NO repair path: both edge tools refuse duplicate_of, /vibe-curate forbids it, redirect_edges has no caller and would double edges if resumed as-is. (b) The cross-clone duplicate-episode case is routine in the separate-clones topology (Stage 2 finding), so the warning will actually fire for teams. Options: (1) implement merge semantics (duplicate_of edge + redirect_edges finished: move edges, remove old ones, tombstone or supersede the duplicate) — real work, needs a spec; (2) delete redirect_edges + the duplicate_of enum value and standardize on `supersedes` as the manual reconciliation edge (cheapest honest state; curate skill already knows supersedes); (3) keep the status quo (detection warns, repair manual via supersedes) and just delete redirect_edges only. Vince's read if useful: option 2 or 3 — duplicate_of has never been usable, supersedes already covers the curator workflow, and an unfinished merge feature is a standing trap; but retiring an enum value shipped in the journal format is a compat question you should sign off on. Workflow-supersedes guardrails are independent and could ship without this decision.

data.summary: Intermittent chromadb rust-backend InternalError flake in test_reconciler_embed_matches_embed_entity_node_directly (2 sightings tonight)
data.detail: The WP-4 parity test test_reconciler_embed_matches_embed_entity_node_directly failed intermittently twice during the 2026-07-02 overnight burn-down full-suite runs, passing in isolation and on immediate re-run both times. First sighting was attributed to mid-edit tree state (gate run while implementer had files half-written) — plausible then. Second sighting (during WP-11, no embeddings/chromadb code in the diff) surfaced actual failure detail: a chromadb rust-backend InternalError — which weakens the mid-edit theory and suggests a real low-frequency flake in the chromadb persistence layer under rapid suite-wide create/write/delete of collections (Windows; pinned chromadb 1.5.5). Neither sighting reproduced under isolation or x3 re-runs; committed content double-green at pins throughout. Investigate: capture the full InternalError next occurrence (run suite with -ra or --lf loop), check if it correlates with test ordering/tmp-dir reuse/handle cleanup, consider a targeted retry marker (pytest-rerunfailures) for the chromadb-touching tests ONLY if root-causing stalls — do not blanket-retry the suite. Related prior art: wp-emb-e4 chromadb rust-lock discovery (SQLite lock contention class).

(1) 8e207087b093: both _atomic_write call sites guarded — OSError → structured write-failed status, clean one-line stderr diagnostic, exit 1; real Windows read-only-file test (empirically verified the mechanism raises before writing the test). (2) 38a5914e6dc6: CognitionStorage/ChromaDBStorage init wrapped like Settings — ERROR log names the failing component+path, then re-raise (log-and-reraise floor; degraded-alive mode deliberately not built); first test_server_lifespan.py, driving lifespan() directly as an async CM. (4) 1a796b2be9b5: bash harness (sanctioned subprocess exception, bash IS the unit) — GATE REDIRECT: v1 built subprocess env via dict(os.environ), inheriting the invoker's PATH; passed 3x for Vorpid, failed 7/8 in Vince's context (cat: command not found) — v2 constructs env from scratch (coreutils dirs derived from the pinned Git Bash exe path, SYSTEMROOT defaulted, per-test vars explicit) + a precondition test that names the derived PATH on failure; verified in two launch contexts. Vorpid self-caught two harness traps: unanchored bash case pattern (*"sync --project"* matched --no-sync too) and subprocess bash resolving to WSL's System32 bash.exe (mounts /mnt/c/, breaking all paths) — pinned to Git Bash explicitly. Lesson (ties to tonight's recurring theme): a test suite that inherits ambient env is only proven for the process shape that ran it — decorrelated gate runs catch what same-context re-runs cannot. Gate: 573 + 9/9 at pin in isolation.

data.detail:
Parent epic for all findings from the 2026-07-01 Fable-coordinated fact-finding audit. Confirmed intended purpose (the audit yardstick): Vibe Cognition is a fully local MCP server plugin for Claude Code that gives a codebase persistent, structured memory — a git-committed knowledge graph (.cognition/journal.jsonl) of decisions, failures, discoveries, constraints, incidents, patterns, workflows, and open tasks — so future Claude Code sessions (and human teammates) understand why the code is the way it is, without re-litigating settled choices or repeating known failures. Primary users are developers using Claude Code daily, and multi-agent / multi-human team collaboration is a first-class use case, not an edge case. Local embeddings (no API keys) power semantic search; the browser dashboard is a secondary, nice-to-have surface — the graph, MCP tools, hooks, and skills are the primary surfaces. Curation is agent-driven via /vibe-curate by current design (it could be automated again in the future, but that is not the current design). Success for a new consumer: install from the marketplace, restart Claude Code, and within a session or two see Claude spontaneously recording history, retrieving relevant context at session start, and — after /vibe-curate — having a browsable, linked graph. Stage docs: docs/260701-fable-audit-systems.md, docs/260701-fable-audit-interconnectivity.md, docs/260701-fable-audit-consumer.md.

--- BURN-DOWN STATUS (Vince, 2026-07-02 overnight) ---
38 of 39 children DONE across 13 work packages (commits d391766..9274515; Vorpid implemented, Vince gated each diff; plan docs/260702-fable-burndown-plan.md / doc:0edbfcc9b8e3), plus 2 pickups: 454226d592e0 (aggregate home-guard gap found mid-run) and 21232d2acaea (H-6(b) --days, folded into WP-13). Version bumped to 0.14.0 with CHANGELOG — RELEASE NOT DONE (human gate: Colton pushes the Loki pin). Sole open child: 4800d5d16adb, parked with a written product question (duplicate_of/redirect_edges fate — options + recommendation in its detail). Close this epic when 4800 resolves.

data.summary: duplicate_of RETIRED + redirect_edges deleted; supersedes is THE reconciliation edge, gaining same-type + acyclic guardrails (Colton delegated; option 2 of the parked 4800d5d16adb question)
data.detail: Colton's morning direction: "go with your best judgement, the path that best maintains graphs and offers the best cognitive result." Chosen: option 2 — remove duplicate_of from CognitionEdgeType and delete CognitionStorage.redirect_edges (zero production callers, would double edges if resumed). Rationale: duplicate_of has been tool-rejected since inception (edge_duplicate_of count = 0; no journal anywhere contains one, making enum retirement compat-safe in practice), and the graph already converged on supersedes as the reconciliation idiom — the curate skill teaches it, WP-12's re-store flow offers it, WP-5's possible_duplicate_of warning suggests it. One idiom beats a permanently-unreachable second one that every future audit re-flags. Folded in: the task's optional guardrails, because supersedes now carries more weight — same-node-type-to-same-node-type enforcement (cross-type supersession is always a modeling error) + cycle prevention via ancestor walk at edge creation (mirroring the task re-parent guard); get_workflow_head's cycle-safety stays as defense in depth. Rejected: option 1 (implement full merge semantics — real feature work with no demonstrated demand; the warned-mint + manual supersedes covers the observed need) and option 3 (keep duplicate_of rejected-but-present — preserves the exact dead-code trap the audit flagged). Ships as WP-14, the final v0.14.0 release blocker.

data.summary: supersedes guardrail REFINED: same-type OR fail/incident-retraction (non-workflow targets only) + acyclic — real journal evidence (fail 352a79a64406 retracting a discovery+decision) overturned the blanket same-type rule
data.detail: Supersedes decision 7b9db5a8d675's guardrail clause. Vorpid's pre-enforcement journal check (required by the WP-14 brief) found 2 of 9 existing supersedes edges are cross-type — fail 352a79a64406 ("FALSE ALARM: SHA-mismatch graph fragmentation does not exist") superseding both the wrong discovery (55e963b39354) and the decision built on it (99b8a9f50164). That is a genuine RETRACTION pattern the graph should encourage: the head of the chain honestly says "this was wrong." Final rule: supersedes allowed iff (a) same node type both ends (newer-version-of-same-thing), OR (b) from-node is fail/incident AND to-node is not a workflow (retraction). Cycle prevention via ancestor walk applies always. The workflow exception exists because workflows are the only type whose retrieval resolves via supersession head (get_workflow_head) — a workflow chain must never resolve to a non-workflow node; a wrong runbook is retracted by a corrected WORKFLOW or a contradicts edge, never by a fail at the head. Both existing cross-type edges are legal under the final rule — no grandfathering needed. Retraction semantics added to the edge-tool docstring tables + edge-analyzer.md so curators learn the pattern. Rejected: blanket same-type (rejects real history), dropping type checks entirely (leaves workflow-head resolution unprotected), grandfathering (a rule that outlaws the graph's own good patterns is the wrong rule).
data.author: Vince

data.reason: Refines the guardrail clause: blanket same-type replaced by same-type-or-retraction after real journal evidence

data.detail: Found by the 2026-07-02 full-curation edgeless sweep: audit stage documents d84cb0405e3b / 90a09a4513b2 / 9a167254861d had ZERO edges even though ~35 tasks cite their doc: refs (e.g. doc:6aaa38e2294f) in references — the deterministic entity→document part_of matcher never linked them, and WP-5's improved startup sweep (which ran on 0.14.0 startup today) didn't backfill them either. Hypotheses to check, in order: (1) cognition_add_task may not run create_deterministic_edges at all (cognition_record does — if task creation skips the matcher, every task citing a doc/commit ref is systematically unlinked); (2) the WP-5 sweep predicate may exclude task nodes or not consider entity→document pairs; (3) doc-ref truncation mismatch between the stored document's reference key and the truncated doc_ref tasks cite (less likely — the burn-down plan doc DID link to its descriptor decision via the same truncated form, but that was cognition_record, supporting hypothesis 1). NOTE: do NOT "fix" by adding task→document part_of edges manually — task part_of is owned by the task-parent hierarchy (skill rule); the right fix is in the matcher/sweep, and it must respect that ownership (probably: link via a non-part_of edge type for tasks, or exempt tasks deliberately and document it). Interim mitigation applied during curation: stage docs manually linked relates_to the epic + led_to the plan doc, so they're no longer invisible.

data.summary: Background curation via plugin-shipped agents CHOSEN (Sonnet curate-orchestrator launched in background, spawning Haiku analyzers); local-model curator daemon PARKED with an A/B reopening gate
data.detail: Colton approved Option B of the background-curation plan (peer-reviewed twice: rework → approve-with-changes, all changes folded in). Design: plugin ships agents/curate-orchestrator.md (model: sonnet, tools: Agent + enumerated cognition_* MCP tools; absorbs the full /vibe-curate pipeline — assess, batch 5-10, spawn Haiku edge-analyzers, review proposals, add_edges_batch with per-edge source, mark_curated per batch, cluster pass, report; embeds the essential analyzer protocol inline so a degraded no-nesting path still works), agents/curate-edge-analyzer.md and agents/curate-cluster-analyzer.md (model: haiku, pinned; bodies = current analyzer .md files). skills/vibe-curate/SKILL.md shrinks to a one-call background launcher with launch-report wording, don't-double-launch line, and get_status uncurated count as ground truth. HONEST MECHANISM (per review): NOT information-hiding — v0.14.0 (task 7d1c151b0372) deliberately put the full edge-semantics table in the add_edge docstrings, so the fix is making the sanctioned path ONE tool call (shorter than the hand-authoring shortcut) with pipeline compliance in a fixed agent system prompt; residual manual-edge risk accepted, mitigated by a light docstring demarcation line (the v0.14.0 table STAYS). REJECTED: Option A local-model daemon (GPT-OSS-20B/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B/Gemma 4 12B on the RTX 4080 16GB box) — re-litigates the v0.7.0 removal (commit 7395672); JSON validity is solved locally (grammar-constrained decoding) but semantic judgment shows ~2x intelligence-index gap vs Haiku class, weakest exactly on supersedes/contradicts/led_to direction; both sibling projects (memcog, linglang-teacher) independently rejected local models and use Gemini 3 Flash API with heavy deterministic engineering; a hosted-model path breaks the plugin's "fully local, no API keys" promise while Option B rides existing Claude Code auth. REOPENING GATE for Option A: A/B eval of a local proposer (with confidence field + Haiku escalation on supersedes/contradicts) vs the shipped pipeline over ~50 real node batches. Mechanics verified: subagent nesting since Claude Code v2.1.172 (depth ≤5); plugin agents proven (vibe-cognition:Plan); backgrounded subagents die with the session (resumable via per-batch mark_curated); hooks cannot spawn detached work. UNVERIFIED → WP-0 spike gates all: background launch of plugin agent, two-level nesting, MCP tool resolution inside, completion notification, old-CC behavior, and whether frontmatter model pins are honored (plan.md uses model: inherit — pinning unproven; silently-ignored haiku pin = cost bug + standing-rule violation).

data.summary: WP-Curate-Background — Final Plan (background curation via plugin-shipped curate-orchestrator agent)
data.detail:
# WP-Curate-Background — Final Plan (APPROVED by Colton 2026-07-02)

Status: FINAL. Approved by Colton; peer-reviewed (general-purpose/sonnet: rework → approve-with-changes, all changes folded). Epic task: 1d7ec9120cef; children WP-0 ebc8f87f6c41, WP-1 d70588cbeeab, WP-2 47055e458727, WP-3 144b6cfc6783, WP-3b 092e7cf26aaa, WP-4 bf7a22dc7294, WP-5 9e399f6e6707, WP-6 db0e6ce1b371. Decision node: ad84223d8fe6. Vorpid implements, Vince gates, Colton executes WP-5, Loki pins WP-6.

## Problem — reframed after review
Main Claude Code agents frequently skip the edge-analyzer subagent during /vibe-curate and hand-author edges, missing the context the analyzer fan-out surfaces.

Root cause (honest version): this is NOT an information-leak problem. As of v0.14.0 (task 7d1c151b0372, commit fb57594 — deliberate, high-severity fix) the full edge-semantics table lives in the cognition_add_edge / cognition_add_edges_batch docstrings, so every agent holding those tools knows how to author edges regardless of what any SKILL.md says. The failure mode is a PROCESS-COMPLIANCE problem: the sanctioned path is a 4-step pipeline the main agent must drive (assess → batch → spawn analyzers → review → commit → cluster), and agents shortcut multi-step processes. The fix is to collapse the sanctioned path to ONE tool call, so the shortcut IS the sanctioned path.

## Evidence gathered
1. Prior art in this repo: v0.7.0 (commit 7395672) REMOVED a background local-LLM auto-curator (CognitionCurator, Ollama qwen3:8b) as "unreliable". Implementation flaws: num_ctx 4096, one node at a time, format:json without schema-constrained decoding, top-8 similarity candidates, in-process daemon thread inside the MCP server, no checkpointing/audit trail.
2. Sibling projects (memcog + linglang-teacher): BOTH use a cheap hosted API model (Gemini 3 Flash), NOT a local model. MemCog evaluated a Claude-based curator and pivoted to Gemini on cost. Both succeed via deterministic engineering: trigger-on-write / session-
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Post-WP-Curate-Background curation containment: ONLY the curate-orchestrator agent may call add_edge/add_edges_batch/mark_curated; all other agents trigger curation via /vibe-curate; orchestrator reports bare counts only
data.detail: Colton's directive during plan approval (effective once WP-Curate-Background ships): semantic curation is fully contained in the plugin-shipped curate-orchestrator agent. (1) The main instance's entire involvement is one background launch call via /vibe-curate plus receiving a success/failure + bare-counts completion message — no edge content, reasons, or node IDs flow back. (2) Tool prohibition with NO manual carve-out: only the curate-orchestrator may use cognition_add_edge, cognition_add_edges_batch, and cognition_mark_curated; even a human-directed one-off edge routes through a curation run. Enforcement is prose-level in the docstrings (callers indistinguishable server-side) — this supersedes the earlier "targeted, user-directed manual edge" allowance. (3) Analyzer subagents are propose-only, enforced by their tool lists (no edge-writing tools). (4) The orchestrator's failure report must say "re-run /vibe-curate to resume", never wording that invites the main agent to finish curation by hand. Rationale: the observed failure mode was main agents skipping the edge-analyzer fan-out and hand-authoring edges; the v0.14.0 edge-semantics docstring table makes information-hiding impossible, so containment + a one-call sanctioned path is the fix.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Background-curation research: both sibling projects (memcog, linglang-teacher) independently rejected local models for graph curation (Gemini 3 Flash + deterministic engineering); local-LLM JSON validity is solved but semantic judgment gaps ~2x vs Haiku class on supersedes/contradicts/led_to
data.detail: Research behind the WP-Curate-Background plan (July 2026). MemCog evaluated a Claude-based curator and pivoted to Gemini 3 Flash on cost; linglang-teacher uses the same model fire-and-forget at session end. Both succeed via engineering, not model choice: checkpointed multi-pass pipelines, strict responseSchema JSON, similarity pre-filtering (≥0.65), confidence gates (≥0.8), caps (≤3 edges/node), fail-open on deterministic 4xx, per-tenant cost ledgers, pass logs; shared scar "bad edges are worse than missing edges". Local-LLM landscape on the RTX 4080 16GB / 64GB box: GPT-OSS-20B (~12GB MXFP4, ~140 tok/s) first-trial candidate, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (MoE CPU-offload) quality ceiling, Gemma 4 12B co-resident option; grammar-constrained decoding (Ollama format schema) guarantees JSON validity, but causal/relational judgment shows roughly a 2x composite-intelligence gap vs Claude Haiku class — weakest exactly on supersedes/contradicts/led_to direction, the heart of edge curation (cluster naming is fine locally). Claude Code mechanics: subagent→subagent nesting since v2.1.172 (depth ≤5); plugin-shipped agents with pinned models proven (vibe-cognition:Plan); backgrounded subagents die with the session; hooks cannot spawn detached work; headless claude -p daemons unsupported. Option A (local-model daemon) parked with reopening gate: A/B of a local proposer + Haiku escalation vs the shipped pipeline over ~50 real node batches.
data.author: Vince

data.detail: Create agents/curate-orchestrator.md (model: sonnet; tools: Agent + enumerated cognition_* MCP tools + Read; system prompt owns the full pipeline — assess via get_status + get_uncurated_nodes(500), batch 5-10 oldest-first, spawn curate-edge-analyzer on haiku per batch, review proposals per current SKILL.md rules, commit via add_edges_batch with per-edge source: curate-skill, mark_curated per batch incl. no-edge nodes, then curate-cluster-analyzer pass, summary nodes + member part_of edges; embeds the essential edge-type/review rules INLINE so a no-nesting degraded path works without reading sibling files; CONTAINMENT: finalizes everything itself, final message = success/failure + bare counts only). Create agents/curate-edge-analyzer.md and agents/curate-cluster-analyzer.md (model: haiku pinned; NO add_edge/add_edges_batch/mark_curated in their tool lists — propose-only). ANALYZER DIRECTIVE HARDENING (Colton, 2026-07-02 — do NOT move edge-analyzer.md verbatim; the 2026-07-02 curation run showed 11 of 26 proposals discarded, and consistent wrong-type proposals mean the prompt needs better directives): (a) SEQUENCING IS NOT CAUSATION — never propose led_to between open sibling tasks in a planned sequence ("WP-2 motivates WP-3" is ordering, not causation; dependency order lives in the parent hierarchy and task detail). led_to requires one node's CONTENT/OUTCOME to have actually produced the other. (b) CHECK EXISTING EDGES FIRST — before proposing, confirm via cognition_get_neighbors that the pair isn't already connected with the same or a stronger type; never re-propose an existing edge. (c) NO SHADOW relates_to — never propose relates_to for a pair already connected (or simultaneously proposed) with any other edge type; relates_to is a last resort for otherwise-unconnected pairs only. (d) TIMESTAMP-CHECK led_to DIRECTION — cause must predate effect; verify node timestamps before proposing. Mirror the same four rules in the orchestrator's review checklist so bad proposals die at review even if an analyzer regresses. Apply WP-0 spike findings (frontmatter shape, model-pin behavior, degradation wording). Blocked by WP-0 (ebc8f87f6c41).

data.summary: Edge-analyzer failure modes (observed 2026-07-02 run, 11/26 proposals discarded): sequencing-as-causation led_to between sibling tasks, re-proposing existing edges, shadow relates_to on already-connected pairs, inverted led_to temporal direction
data.detail: A live /vibe-curate run over the WP-Curate-Background planning nodes (13 nodes, 2 Haiku edge-analyzer batches) discarded 11 of 26 proposals, all falling into four repeatable failure modes: (1) SEQUENCING-AS-CAUSATION — 8 led_to edges proposed between open sibling WP tasks ("WP-2 motivates WP-3"); planned execution order is not causation, and the parent epic's part_of hierarchy already carries ordering. (2) DUPLICATE RE-PROPOSAL — parallel batches each proposed the same cross-batch edges (idempotency caught it, but the analysis tokens were wasted). (3) SHADOW relates_to — a weak relates_to proposed on a pair simultaneously proposed (or already connected) with a stronger led_to. (4) INVERTED led_to DIRECTION — cause node postdating the effect node (constraint recorded at 20:57 proposed as leading to a task created at 20:36). Countermeasures folded into WP-1 (d70588cbeeab) as analyzer directive hardening: never led_to between open sibling tasks; check cognition_get_neighbors for existing edges before proposing; relates_to only for otherwise-unconnected pairs; timestamp-check led_to direction. The orchestrator's review checklist mirrors all four so bad proposals die at review even if an analyzer regresses. General lesson: when an analyzer subagent consistently returns a wrong CATEGORY of proposal, fix the analyzer's directives — don't just rely on the reviewer discarding them forever.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: No dev-loaded plugin exists on this machine; vibe-cognition runs from a pinned marketplace cache — `--plugin-dir <path>` is the correct session-scoped dev-load mechanism for spike-testing new agent files
data.detail: WP-0 spike, step 1. The plan/task assumed a "dev-loaded plugin is fine for all checks" testing path. That path doesn't exist here: installed_plugins.json shows vibe-cognition@coltondyck installed at user scope from the marketplace cache (C:\Users\colto\.claude\plugins\cache\coltondyck\vibe-cognition\0.14.0, pinned to commit 811350d1), which mirrors exactly what's committed on main — editing agents/*.md in the live repo working tree has zero effect on any running or freshly-spawned session, because Claude Code reads plugin content from that cache snapshot, not the repo. Confirmed by adding a throwaway agent (zzspike-orchestrator.md) to the repo's agents/ dir and finding it absent from 'Available agents' both in the live interactive session AND in a brand-new headless `claude -p` process launched from the repo directory. The correct tool for testing new/unshipped agent files without touching the shared installed cache is the CLI flag `claude --plugin-dir <path>` (also `--plugin-url`), which loads a plugin directory for that single session only. This is what made the rest of the WP-0 spike possible.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: A live Claude Code session's agent registry is snapshotted at process start — new/edited agent .md files are invisible to that session even mid-run; only a fresh process (or, in production, a real plugin update + restart) picks them up
data.detail: WP-0 spike, step 1 corollary. Confirms the practical implication of the previous discovery: shipping new agent files (WP-1/WP-2/WP-6) will require the user's session to restart (or the marketplace-pinned plugin to be reinstalled/updated and the session relaunched) before the new curate-orchestrator/curate-edge-analyzer/curate-cluster-analyzer agents become invokable. This should be called out explicitly in WP-5's human release checklist (task 9e399f6e6707) if not already implied by the existing "extend the hands-on release checklist" language — the checklist should make clear that a stale, still-running session will NOT see the new agents even after `/plugin update` completes; a fresh session is required.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: Two-level plugin-agent nesting (Sonnet orchestrator spawning a Haiku analyzer via the Agent tool) works cleanly — confirmed 3/3 runs via --plugin-dir dev-load, including a collision-free rerun
data.detail: WP-0 spike, step 2. Built two throwaway plugin agents (ZZSpikeOrchestrator: model sonnet, tools Agent+Read+MCP; ZZSpikeAnalyzer: model haiku, tools MCP-only) and ran them via `claude -p --plugin-dir <repo> --dangerously-skip-permissions`, instructing the orchestrator to spawn the analyzer synchronously (run_in_background: false) via the Agent tool with subagent_type "<plugin>:ZZSpikeAnalyzer". NESTING_RESULT was "yes, succeeded, no error" in all 3 runs (one with the real plugin name colliding with the installed vibe-cognition@coltondyck, two more after renaming to a collision-free "vibe-cognition-zzspike" scratch plugin pointed at the same server code). This directly confirms the epic's core mechanic (curate-orchestrator sonnet -> curate-edge-analyzer/curate-cluster-analyzer haiku, two-level nesting) is sound on this Claude Code version (2.1.198, comfortably above the documented v2.1.172 nesting floor). GO signal for WP-1's core design.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: Frontmatter model: pins ARE honored for plugin-shipped agents, isolated from any explicit model override — resolves the "unproven, plan.md only uses model:inherit" concern flagged by the plan
data.detail: WP-0 spike, step 3 (the item flagged highest-risk: "a silently-ignored haiku pin is a cost bug + standing-rule violation"). Isolated test: orchestrator's own system prompt was rewritten to explicitly NOT pass a `model` parameter on its Agent() call to the analyzer, so the ONLY thing that could determine the analyzer's model was ZZSpikeAnalyzer.md's own frontmatter `model: haiku` line. Result across both the colliding-name and collision-free reruns: orchestrator consistently reported running as "Sonnet 5 / claude-sonnet-5" (its own frontmatter model: sonnet, session default), and the analyzer consistently self-reported "Haiku 4.5 / claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" with MODEL_PARAM_PASSED explicitly confirmed as not-passed. Frontmatter model pins for plugin agents are real and effective, not silently ignored. GO signal — WP-1 can rely on model: haiku in curate-edge-analyzer.md / curate-cluster-analyzer.md without the orchestrator needing to pass an explicit override as a safety net (though doing so anyway costs nothing and is a reasonable belt-and-suspenders addition).
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: MCP tool resolution inside --plugin-dir-loaded plugin agents was FLAKY across spike runs (worked once with genuine data, failed 3 other times) — most likely an MCP-server cold-start race specific to the spike's fresh-process test method, not a frontmatter-declaration bug, but NOT independently confirmed as production-safe
data.detail: WP-0 spike, step 3 continued — the one finding that keeps this from being an unqualified GO. Across 4 measurements (2 runs x orchestrator+analyzer, plus a dedicated warm-up-first rerun): the analyzer's declared MCP tool (get_status) resolved and returned real, verifiable data exactly once (matched this graph's actual live stats: 385 nodes/604 edges) — every other measurement, including the orchestrator in all 3 runs and the analyzer in the warm-up rerun, reported the declared MCP tool as TOOL_NOT_IN_LIST despite correct frontmatter syntax (mcp__plugin_<name>_<server>__<tool>) and correct plugin.json mcpServers config. A dedicated warm-up test (main session calls the MCP tool itself first, via ToolSearch retries, before spawning the orchestrator) still failed and confirmed via ToolSearch that the zzspike MCP server's tools were never exposed to the main session at all in that run — ruling out a simple "session hadn't started the server yet" story and pointing instead at per-invocation variability in whether `--plugin-dir`'s fresh `uv run` MCP server process finishes connecting before the harness snapshots that session's/subagent's tool list. This is very plausibly a testing-artifact of `--plugin-dir` cold-starting a brand-new server process on every single headless invocation — in real production use the vibe-cognition MCP server is already a long-running, warm process from session start, well before /vibe-curate would ever launch the orchestrator — but that theory is NOT independently verified. RECOMMENDATION (elevates rather than blocks): (1) WP-4's in-repo verification must treat MCP-resolution-inside-the-orchestrator as its primary, non-optional check, run against the actually-installed warm plugin, not just "spawn analyzers" — don't treat a clean transcript as sufficient if it doesn't confirm real (non-fabricated) MCP tool results at both nesting levels; (2) WP-1's orchestrator (and analyzer) system prompts should include a cheap defensive line: if a declared MCP tool is unexpectedly absent from your own tool list, or a call errors, STOP and report failure rather than silently proceeding or fabricating results — the spike also caught a Haiku analyzer fabricating a plausible-looking successful get_status result with 0 real tool_uses on an earlier, less-instrumented prompt revision, which is a real hallucination risk worth guarding against explicitly in the shipped prompts, not just the throwaway spike prompts.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: WP-0 Spike complete: GO on WP-Curate-Background, with two required adjustments (orchestrator anti-hallucination/MCP-absence guard; WP-4 must treat MCP resolution as its primary check)
data.detail:
Ran the WP-0 spike (task ebc8f87f6c41) for the WP-Curate-Background epic (1d7ec9120cef, decision ad84223d8fe6). Built two throwaway plugin agents (ZZSpikeOrchestrator: sonnet, Agent+Read+MCP tools; ZZSpikeAnalyzer: haiku, MCP-only) and tested via `claude -p --plugin-dir <path> --dangerously-skip-permissions`, since (discovery ffd6cc84bef7) no dev-loaded plugin exists on this machine — vibe-cognition is a pinned marketplace-cache install, and a live/fresh session doesn't pick up new agent files without a process restart (discovery a8404e70d14a). Iterated through a name-collision issue (renamed to a scratch plugin "vibe-cognition-zzspike" pointed at the same server code to get a clean read) and a prompt-hardening pass after catching a Haiku analyzer fabricate a plausible get_status result with 0 real tool_uses.

Results against the 6 checks in the task: (1) background launch — not separately spiked, proven everyday mechanic already; (2) two-level nesting — CONFIRMED working 3/3 runs (discovery f6d4bfde0cfc); (3) MCP tools resolve inside both levels — FLAKY, worked once with verified-genuine data, failed 3 other times, most likely a --plugin-dir cold-start race rather than a design flaw, but not independently confirmed safe in production (discovery c98a5f3b6d7f, severity high); (4) completion notification reaches the launcher — not separately spiked, proven mechanic; (5) old-CC (<v2.1.172) behavior — untestable, this machine only has 2.1.198 installed; (6) frontmatter model pins honored — CONFIRMED via an isolated test with no explicit model override on the nested Agent() call (discovery d79cd9a93a02).

GO/NO-GO: GO on the epic design as specified. Two adjustments recommended before/during WP-1 and WP-4 (not design changes, just hardening + verification emphasis): (a) curate-orchestrator.md and curate-edge-analyzer.md/curate-cluster-analyzer.md system prompts should include an explicit "if a declared MCP tool is absent from your tool list or a call errors, STOP and report failure — never fabricate a result" line; (b) WP-4's in-repo verification (task bf7a22dc7294) must specifically confirm real, non-fabricated MCP tool results at both nesting levels against the actually-installed warm plugin, not just a clean-looking transcript. No changes needed to the WP-1/WP-2/WP-3/WP-3b/WP-5/WP-6 design itself. Cleaned up: deleted the throwaway agents/zzspike-*.md from the repo working tree (repo status confirmed clean apart from the routine .cognition/journal.jsonl delta); the scratch collision-free plugin remains only in the session scratchpad, never touched the repo or the installed plugin cache.

data.reason: Discovery of --plugin-dir dev-load mechanism directly led to the corollary discovery about session agent registry snapshots and fresh-process implications.
Discovery that --plugin-dir is the correct testing mechanism enabled the spike's step 2 to successfully test two-level plugin-agent nesting using that exact mechanism.
Discovery that the agent registry snapshots at process start directly motivated the spike to run fresh sessions via claude -p --plugin-dir to correctly test the nesting design.
