Discovery of MCP tool resolution flakiness directly led to the episode's two critical hardening recommendations: MCP-absence guards in system prompts and mandatory WP-4 verification of real MCP results at both nesting levels.

data.detail: Live run against this repo's real uncurated nodes. PRIMARY CHECK (WP-0 adjustment, episode 1859e72ab7f9 — MCP resolution inside nested plugin agents was FLAKY under --plugin-dir, discovery c98a5f3b6d7f severity high, likely cold-start race but unproven in production): confirm REAL, non-fabricated MCP tool results at BOTH nesting levels against the actually-installed warm plugin — verify actual tool_uses counts in transcripts and cross-check returned data against the graph (a clean-looking transcript is not evidence; a spike Haiku analyzer fabricated a plausible get_status result with 0 real tool_uses). Secondary checks: orchestrator launched in background while main session stays responsive; orchestrator transcript shows Haiku edge-analyzer subagents actually spawned (not inline shortcut); edges committed with source=curate-skill; mark_curated per batch; cluster pass ran; final message back to main = success/failure + bare counts only (containment holds — no edge content leaks into the main transcript); smoke-check the verification run produced no hand-authored edges by the MAIN agent (single-sample smoke test, not proof); kill-the-session-mid-run test — relaunch and confirm resume picks up only the remainder (idempotent mark_curated). Blocked by WP-2/WP-3.

Create agents/curate-orchestrator.md (model: sonnet; tools: Agent + enumerated cognition_* MCP tools + Read; system prompt owns the full pipeline — assess via get_status + get_uncurated_nodes(500), batch 5-10 oldest-first, spawn curate-edge-analyzer on haiku per batch, review proposals per current SKILL.md rules, commit via add_edges_batch with per-edge source: curate-skill, mark_curated per batch incl. no-edge nodes, then curate-cluster-analyzer pass, summary nodes + member part_of edges; embeds the essential edge-type/review rules INLINE so a no-nesting degraded path works without reading sibling files; CONTAINMENT: finalizes everything itself, final message = success/failure + bare counts only). Create agents/curate-edge-analyzer.md and agents/curate-cluster-analyzer.md (model: haiku pinned; NO add_edge/add_edges_batch/mark_curated in their tool lists — propose-only). ANALYZER DIRECTIVE HARDENING (Colton, 2026-07-02 — do NOT move edge-analyzer.md verbatim; the 2026-07-02 curation run showed 11 of 26 proposals discarded, and consistent wrong-type proposals mean the prompt needs better directives): (a) SEQUENCING IS NOT CAUSATION — never propose led_to between open sibling tasks in a planned sequence ("WP-2 motivates WP-3" is ordering, not causation; dependency order lives in the parent hierarchy and task detail). led_to requires one node's CONTENT/OUTCOME to have actually produced the other. (b) CHECK EXISTING EDGES FIRST — before proposing, confirm via cognition_get_neighbors that the pair isn't already connected with the same or a stronger type; never re-propose an existing edge. (c) NO SHADOW relates_to — never propose relates_to for a pair already connected (or simultaneously proposed) with any other edge type; relates_to is a last resort for otherwise-unconnected pairs only. (d) TIMESTAMP-CHECK led_to DIRECTION — cause must predate effect; verify node timestamps before proposing. Mirror the same four rules in the orchestrator's review checklist so bad proposals die at review even if an analyzer regresses. ANTI-FABRICATION GUARD (WP-0 adjustment, episode 1859e72ab7f9 — a spike Haiku analyzer fabricated a plausible get_status result with 0 real tool_uses): ALL THREE agent prompts include an explicit line: "if a declared MCP tool is absent from your tool list or a call errors, STOP and report failure — never fabricate a result." Apply remaining WP-0 spike findings (frontmatter shape confirmed, model pins confirmed honored d79cd9a93a02, degradation wording stays prompt-level since old-CC was untestable). Blocked by WP-0 (ebc8f87f6c41) — CLEARED, gated GO by Vince.

data.summary: WP-1 implemented: three curation agent files added (curate-orchestrator sonnet, curate-edge-analyzer + curate-cluster-analyzer haiku-pinned, propose-only) with containment, anti-fabrication guard, and analyzer directive hardening baked in — awaiting Vince's gate
data.detail:
Implemented task d70588cbeeab for the WP-Curate-Background epic (1d7ec9120cef). Added agents/curate-orchestrator.md (model: sonnet; tools: Agent, Read + 8 enumerated cognition_* MCP tools), agents/curate-edge-analyzer.md and agents/curate-cluster-analyzer.md (model: haiku, pinned; propose-only tool lists — no add_edge/add_edges_batch/mark_curated/cognition_record on either analyzer, per the epic's forceful demarcation).

Orchestrator absorbs the current skills/vibe-curate/SKILL.md pipeline (assess -> batch 5-10 oldest-first -> spawn curate-edge-analyzer on haiku (no explicit model override, relying on the WP-0-confirmed frontmatter pin, discovery d79cd9a93a02) -> review -> commit via add_edges_batch (source: curate-skill) -> mark_curated per batch -> spawn curate-cluster-analyzer -> review -> create summary nodes + part_of edges), with three changes: (1) CONTAINMENT — final message to the launcher is bare success/failure + counts only, no edge content/reasons/node IDs, replacing the old narrated Step 4 report; (2) ANTI-FABRICATION GUARD (WP-0 adjustment) — explicit STOP-and-report-don't-fabricate line in all three prompts, plus the orchestrator is told not to trust an ungrounded-looking subagent proposal at face value; (3) the four 2026-07-02 analyzer-hardening directives (sequencing != causation, check-neighbors-before-proposing, no-shadow-relates_to, timestamp-check led_to direction — pattern 4118b9ab0787) are in the edge-analyzer's own rules AND mirrored in the orchestrator's review checklist, so a regressed or degraded analyzer still gets caught at review. Degraded/no-nesting fallback: if Agent is genuinely unavailable, the orchestrator has an embedded condensed analyzer protocol to run inline rather than blocking the whole run. Failure wording is explicitly "re-run /vibe-curate to resume" — never anything inviting the main instance to finish by hand.

Also added one hardening not explicitly requested but directly informed by a WP-0 spike incident: the cluster-analyzer's summary-node-detail guidance now says to verify each member's actual status/role from the node itself (not infer from summary text), because the WP-0 spike's own cluster-analyzer test mischaracterized a done task as an open one.

Verified via `claude -p --plugin-dir <repo>` (same collision-tolerant dev-load method proven in WP-0) that all three new agents register correctly with their intended tool lists — this is a lint-level sanity check only, not the functional/MCP-resolution verification, which is explicitly WP-4's job (bf7a22dc7294) and deliberately not duplicated here. Did not touch skills/vibe-curate/*.md (that's WP-2) or any docstrings (WP-3/3b). Did not commit — left for Vince per the sequential-on-main / journal-stays-with-manager protocol; repo status is clean except the three new untracked files and the routine journal delta.

data.detail:
Applied Vince's three WP-3+3b gate fixes: (1) add_edge docstring's DOCUMENT nodes paragraph reframed from "intentionally manually-linkable" to "authored by the curate-orchestrator like any other semantic edge" per Vince's FOLD ruling (not carve-out) — substance (supersedes doc-to-doc versioning, matcher auto-link scope, re-mint never overwrites) preserved verbatim; (2) skills/vibe-document/SKILL.md frontmatter inverted to launch-semantics ("Curation is YOUR job to TRIGGER... never author semantic edges yourself"); (3) skills/vibe-document/SKILL.md Step 3 body reframed ("run /vibe-curate to launch the background curator, which adds the semantic edges").

Then swept skills/ + src/ per Vince's instruction ("trust the grep, not my list" — his original nine-item audit missed vibe-document entirely). Found and fixed 4 more genuine misses his list didn't cover: skills/vibe-backfill/SKILL.md:134 ("run /vibe-curate to create semantic edges" -> launch-semantics); skills/vibe-cognition/SKILL.md's own tool reference table row for cognition_add_edge/cognition_add_edges_batch (still said "Manually create an edge" with no restriction noted — added the ONLY-the-curate-orchestrator line to both rows). Also went one step beyond the literal "skills/ + src/" instruction, since the same grep surfaced two factually-false claims in the repo-root README.md ("There is no automated background curator" in the Curation section, and "Curation is agent-driven: ... run /vibe-curate to create semantic edges" in Troubleshooting) — fixed both to launch-semantics since they're the same class of now-false claim and README.md is a tested surface (test_doc_drift.py's node/edge-type presence guards read it). Flagging this scope extension rather than silently doing it: Vince's instruction was skills/+src/ specifically, README.md is neither, but leaving a known-false claim in the most user-facing doc in the repo seemed worse than a small scope creep.

Re-ran full test suite (618 passed, 1 skipped) and ruff check (clean) after all fixes — no regressions. Did not commit — still waiting on Vince's full-diff review. Proceeding to WP-4 per his go-ahead in the same gate message.

data.summary: WP-4 verification approach: synchronous --plugin-dir run against real uncurated nodes for the primary MCP-resolution check, since headless print-mode's true backgrounding semantics are unproven and risk orphaning the orchestrator mid-run
data.detail: Graph had 0 uncurated nodes when WP-4 started, so ran this decision itself uncurated as genuine test fodder rather than fabricating throwaway content. Chose run_in_background:false for the orchestrator inside a single `claude -p --plugin-dir <repo> --dangerously-skip-permissions` headless invocation: a print-mode process exits after its top-level turn, and whether a backgrounded child agent survives that exit is untested — orphaning the orchestrator mid-curation would be worse than just not testing true backgrounding here. The background-launch-and-stay-responsive mechanic itself is not re-tested as novel (it's the same everyday Agent(run_in_background=true) mechanic used constantly in ordinary interactive sessions, already trusted); this WP-4 run instead focuses its scrutiny on the PRIMARY check the epic actually flagged as unresolved: real vs fabricated MCP tool results at both nesting levels, against this repo's real (not scratch/collision-renamed) plugin content, which WP-0's spike could not fully settle.

data.summary: WP-0's flaky --plugin-dir MCP resolution did NOT reproduce in a real production-file WP-4 run: raw subagent transcripts show 18 (orchestrator) + 14 (edge-analyzer) + 37 (cluster-analyzer) genuine mcp__plugin_vibe-cognition_vibe-cognition__* tool_use calls, cross-checked against ground-truth graph state changes
data.detail:
WP-4 primary check. Ran the real (non-scratch-renamed) agents/curate-orchestrator.md via `claude -p --plugin-dir <repo> --dangerously-skip-permissions --session-id <known-uuid>` against 1 genuine uncurated node (decision 7b4cff556f90). Went beyond trusting the orchestrator's self-report (the whole point of this WP, per WP-0's own fabrication catch): read the RAW on-disk subagent JSONL transcripts directly from ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-path>/<session-id>/subagents/ and counted actual tool_use content blocks. Orchestrator (spawnDepth 1, agent-a36f3288934fba77a.jsonl): 18 real tool_use events incl. get_status, cognition_get_uncurated_nodes, TWO Agent calls (spawning both analyzers, confirmed via their .meta.json files: agentType curate-edge-analyzer + curate-cluster-analyzer, both spawnDepth 2 — real nesting, not inline fallback), cognition_add_edges_batch, cognition_mark_curated. Edge-analyzer: 14 real tool_use events (get_neighbors, get_node x4, search x3, get_history, more get_neighbors) with tool_result payloads matching genuine graph content (real node ids/summaries verifiable against the actual graph, not plausible-looking invention). Cluster-analyzer: 37 real tool_use events building its candidate pool and reviewing.

Ground-truth cross-check: get_status before/after (edges 644->647, edge_led_to 107->110 = exactly +3, matching the orchestrator's claimed "3 edges created"), and cognition_get_neighbors on 7b4cff556f90 directly (independently, not via the orchestrator) showed exactly 3 new incoming led_to edges with source-appropriate, contextually correct reasons citing real related nodes (ad84223d8fe6, c98a5f3b6d7f, 1859e72ab7f9) — content a fabricating model could not plausibly invent without real search/neighbor access. uncurated dropped 1->0 (mark_curated fired for real).

Conclusion: WP-0's flaky MCP resolution under --plugin-dir was very likely a cold-start-race artifact of that spike's own test methodology (rapid iteration on a scratch collision-renamed plugin, sometimes hitting the server before it fully connected), not a fundamental flaw in the real agents/curate-orchestrator.md design. Does not yet cover the INSTALLED-cache scenario (WP-5, Colton's machine, per Vince's acceptance).
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: WP-4 caught a real containment violation: curate-orchestrator's shipped Final Report section wasn't strict enough — its actual output was a numbered Step-1/2/3 narrative naming a discarded cluster's subject matter and its own review reasoning, not the bare-counts-only line the prompt example showed
data.detail: Same WP-4 verification run as discovery [MCP resolution not fabricated]. The orchestrator's real final message was: \"Step 1 — Assess: 397 nodes / 644 edges... Step 2 — Edge curation: 1 batch (1 node) processed via curate-edge-analyzer, all 3 proposed led_to edges passed review (timestamp order, no duplicates, real causal evidence)... Step 3 — Cluster pass: curate-cluster-analyzer proposed 1 candidate episode summarizing a 3-incident shared-worktree-safety arc. On review, all 3 member nodes were already fully interconnected...\" — three independent containment breaches in one message: (a) a numbered step-by-step structure instead of one summary line, (b) naming what the discarded cluster proposal was ABOUT (the shared-worktree-safety topic), (c) narrating its own review reasoning (timestamp/duplicate/causal-evidence checks) instead of just a pass/fail count. A single good bare-counts EXAMPLE in the prompt was not sufficient guardrail — the model still defaulted to a helpful, thorough narrative.\n\nFix applied to agents/curate-orchestrator.md: added a concrete BAD example (this exact real output, verbatim) alongside the good one, explained why each of the three parts is a violation, and added an explicit self-check checklist before sending the final message (no \"Step N\" labels, no naming what a discarded item was about, no explaining pass/fail reasoning, hard 1-2 sentence cap). Not yet re-verified with a live run — that's the next WP-4 step.

data.detail:
Full WP-4 run sequence against this repo's real uncurated nodes via `claude -p --plugin-dir <repo> --dangerously-skip-permissions`:

1. First live run (session ca0a18aa): PRIMARY CHECK PASSED with strongest possible evidence — read raw on-disk subagent JSONL transcripts directly (not the orchestrator's self-report) and counted genuine tool_use events: orchestrator 18, edge-analyzer 14, cluster-analyzer 37, all real mcp__plugin_vibe-cognition_vibe-cognition__* calls with tool_result payloads matching actual graph content. Ground-truth cross-check (get_status + get_neighbors called independently by the test harness, not the orchestrator) confirmed exactly the edges/counts claimed. Full detail in discovery 92ce0ec84f67. WP-0's flaky MCP resolution did not reproduce.

2. Same run caught a genuine CONTAINMENT VIOLATION: the orchestrator's real final message was a numbered Step-1/2/3 narrative naming a discarded cluster's subject matter and its own review reasoning — not the bare-counts-only line its prompt example specified. Full detail in fail d537efeee17e. Fixed agents/curate-orchestrator.md's Final Report + CONTAINMENT sections: added the actual bad output as a concrete counter-example, explained each of its three violations, added a pre-send self-check checklist, hard 1-2 sentence cap.

3. Kill-mid-run resume test (sessions 3b87030a killed, then 3e6da3b8 resumed): launched curation, waited for the orchestrator subagent to actually spawn (confirmed via its .meta.json appearing on disk), then TaskStop-killed the whole process immediately. Verified via get_status that NOTHING committed (edges unchanged, uncurated still 2) — clean interruption, no partial/corrupt state. Relaunched fresh: resumed cleanly, processed the same 2 nodes to completion (uncurated 2->0, edges +2 matching the orchestrator's claim exactly), and its final message this time was properly terse bare-counts ("Curation complete. 2 uncurated nodes processed -> 2 edges created, 0 proposals discarded -> cluster pass found 0 new clusters worth summarizing -> uncurated count is now 0.") — confirms the containment fix works, not just in isolation but under real resumed-run conditions.

NEW CAVEAT (not yet investigated, flagging for follow-up): the resume run's outer test-harness session reported the orchestrator's Agent() call returning an INTERMEDIATE status on its first response rather than running straight to completion, requiring the harness to send a follow-up nudge ("finish reviewing/committing edges, run the cluster pass, and report") before getting the real final message. Unclear whether this is (a) an artifact of headless print-mode's own turn/response boundaries for a very long-running synchronous Agent() call, (b) something about how the specific test prompt was worded, or (c) a genuine risk that the orchestrator could stall mid-pipeline in real background usage without anyone there to nudge it. Needs investigation before WP-4 can be called fully clean — this is the most important open item for the next session.

STILL UNVERIFIED at the pause point: (a) edges committed with source=curate-skill specifically (get_neighbors doesn't expose the source field; inferred-but-not-directly-confirmed so far — could check via the journal or a dedicated read); (b) degraded/no-nesting inline-mode fallback path (not exercised in any run so far, all had Agent available); (c) the installed-cache (non-plugin-dir) scenario, explicitly deferred to WP-5 per Vince's acceptance.

PAUSED here on Vince's urgent [BLOCKER] hold (2026-07-02T16:17): Colton's usage limits approaching, WP-4 is the token-heaviest WP in the epic. No new orchestrator/analyzer spawns after this point — the in-flight resume-verification run above was allowed to finish per his instruction, nothing new started since. Resume plan when cleared: (1) investigate the intermediate-stop caveat, (2) verify source=curate-skill directly, (3) exercise the degraded-mode fallback if practical, (4) then report full WP-4 findings to Vince for gate.

data.summary: WP-4 stall investigation: test true backgrounding (Agent tool, run_in_background:true) from a genuinely persistent interactive session rather than headless -p, since a -p process exiting after its top-level turn could orphan a truly backgrounded child — using general-purpose+full curate-orchestrator prompt inline (agent not registered in this live session) against this session's own real, already-warm installed MCP tools
data.detail: Per Vince's instruction to distinguish a headless-print-mode artifact from a genuine background-launch stall. This live Vorpid session is a real, persistent interactive session (unlike the claude -p invocations used for the primary MCP-resolution check) and already has the real installed vibe-cognition MCP tools warm and proven reliable all session — but doesn't have the new curate-orchestrator agent type registered (discovery a8404e70d14a: new plugin agent files aren't picked up mid-session). Workaround: launch the existing general-purpose agent type (full tool access incl. Agent + all MCP tools) with curate-orchestrator.md's complete system prompt pasted inline as its task, via Agent(run_in_background=true) — mechanically identical to what launching the real named agent would do (same nested Agent() call to spawn a Haiku edge-analyzer/cluster-analyzer, same MCP tools, same review logic), differing only in the top-level agent's registered name/type, which shouldn't affect whether a long multi-step pipeline stalls mid-run needing an external nudge. If this completes cleanly via the normal task-notification mechanism with no nudge needed, that points to the earlier stall being a -p/headless artifact specific to that test method, not a fundamental flaw in real background-agent usage.

data.summary: WP-4 stall caveat RESOLVED: the intermediate-status/nudge-needed behavior was a headless `claude -p` artifact — the identical pipeline launched via a genuine interactive Agent(run_in_background=true) call completed cleanly via the normal task-notification with zero intervention, correct bare-counts final message, and ground-truth-verified real edges
data.detail:
Per Vince's instruction to distinguish a headless-print-mode artifact from a real background-launch design flaw (decision a7682efb5795 for the test methodology). Launched the full curate-orchestrator pipeline (pasted inline into a general-purpose agent, since the named agent isn't registered in this live session — mechanically identical: real MCP tools, real nested Agent() call to a Haiku sub-analyzer, same review/commit logic) via Agent(subagent_type="general-purpose", model="sonnet", run_in_background=true) from this genuinely persistent interactive Vorpid session — the real end-user launch pattern /vibe-curate uses, not a simulated one-shot headless process.

Result: ran fully autonomously for ~321s, completed via the standard task-notification with NO nudge, NO intervention, NO polling required beyond normal notification wait. Final message was properly bare-counts and containment-compliant: "Curation complete. 1 uncurated node processed -> 2 edges created, 0 proposals discarded, 0 clusters found -> 0 summary nodes created." Ground-truth verified via independent cognition_get_neighbors + get_status calls: exactly 2 new led_to edges landed on the test decision node (a7682efb5795), with specific, contextually correct reasoning, uncurated confirmed 0.

Conclusion: the earlier stall (episode c8415d1301b5) was specific to the `claude -p` headless test harness used for the primary MCP-resolution check — most likely related to how a single synchronous Agent() call behaves across very long tool-heavy turns inside a one-shot print-mode process, not a property of real background-agent usage in an actual Claude Code session. This item is now closed as a harness artifact, not a release blocker. The primary-check verification method (headless -p) remains valid for what it tested (real vs. fabricated MCP calls); it just wasn't the right method for testing true backgrounding, which this discovery now covers via the correct method instead.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: WP-4 degraded-mode test attempt 1: the scratch test plugin's MCP server itself failed to connect (same --plugin-dir cold-start flakiness as WP-0), so it didn't exercise the intended "no Agent, cognition tools present" scenario — but it DID prove the anti-fabrication guard works correctly under a genuine total-tool-absence failure: no fabrication, clear failure report, correct "re-run to resume" wording
data.detail: Built a scratch plugin (scratchpad/degraded-test-plugin, name vibe-cognition-degradedtest, non-colliding, pointed at the real vibe_cognition server code) with a copy of agents/curate-orchestrator.md that has Agent removed from its tools list, to test the degraded/no-nesting inline fallback path per Vince's WP-4 resume instruction item 3. First run: the ENTIRE degradedtest MCP server failed to connect for both the outer test session and the orchestrator subagent — ToolSearch found nothing, direct tool invocation errored \"No such tool available\", ListMcpResourcesTool showed zero vibe-cognition resources. This is the same MCP-server cold-start race pattern documented in WP-0 (discovery c98a5f3b6d7f) recurring under a fresh scratch --plugin-dir invocation.\n\nThis meant the orchestrator had ZERO cognition tools (not the intended \"cognition tools present, Agent absent\" scenario) — but its behavior under that real failure was exactly correct per its anti-fabrication guard: verbatim final message was \"I checked my available tool list before starting the pipeline... none of these are present... Per the anti-fabrication guard, I will not simulate or fabricate results for tools I don't have access to. Curation could not run: required cognition tools are unavailable in this session. Re-run /vibe-curate to resume.\" No fabrication, no fake counts, correct failure wording, no invitation to hand-fix. This is real, valuable evidence the anti-fabrication guard (WP-0 adjustment) works as designed under an actual failure, even though this specific run doesn't settle the degraded-mode question. Retrying with an explicit multi-attempt MCP warm-up before the real test, per Vince's \"don't over-invest, one run suffices\" guidance applied to the RETRY, not this failed attempt.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: WP-4 degraded-mode test: 2/2 attempts failed to exercise the intended scenario because the scratch MCP server never connected either time — anti-fabrication guard proved correct 2/2 regardless; refined hypothesis is venv-reuse contention with this live session's own concurrently-running real server, not a pure cold-start timing race; stopped retrying per "don't over-invest"
data.detail:
Both degraded-mode test attempts (scratch plugin vibe-cognition-degradedtest, Agent removed from curate-orchestrator's tools, pointed at the real server code) failed identically: the scratch MCP server exposed ZERO tools to either the outer test session or the orchestrator subagent, confirmed via ToolSearch, direct invocation, and ListMcpResourcesTool all coming up empty (not "still connecting", genuinely absent). Attempt 2 added an explicit multi-retry warm-up before launching the orchestrator and still got nothing. So neither run actually tested "cognition tools present, Agent absent" — both tested "zero tools present" instead.

What WAS proven, twice independently: the anti-fabrication guard behaves exactly as designed under a real, unplanned total-tool-absence failure — both orchestrator runs detected the gap, refused to fabricate a result, and returned the correct "could not run / re-run to resume" failure message with no invitation to hand-fix. This is real evidence the guard works under actual failure conditions, not just the happy path.

REFINED HYPOTHESIS for the underlying MCP flakiness (updates discovery c98a5f3b6d7f from WP-0): both this test's scratch plugin AND WP-0's zzspike scratch plugin pointed their UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT at the SAME already-in-use venv (~/.claude/plugins/data/vibe-cognition-coltondyck/.venv) that this live Vorpid session's own real, continuously-running vibe-cognition MCP server was concurrently using throughout. A second `uv run` process attempting to start against a venv another live process already holds could plausibly hit lock/contention behavior that prevents clean tool exposure within the resolution window — this would explain the flakiness being worse here (2/2 failures) than in WP-0's PRIMARY-check runs (which used a fresh --plugin-dir pointed at the REAL repo's own default venv resolution, not a manually-reused shared path) and would mean this specific failure mode is a testing-methodology artifact (concurrent venv sharing with an already-running server), not present in real production use where the installed orchestrator's server IS the only server using that venv. NOT independently confirmed — a genuine open question, not a settled conclusion.

STOPPED after 2 attempts per Vince's explicit "don't over-invest, one run suffices" guidance (already doubled that). Net effect on WP-4: the degraded/no-nesting inline-execution PATH itself (does the orchestrator correctly self-perform edge analysis when Agent is absent but cognition tools work) remains functionally UNVERIFIED — this is a real gap, not resolved, being reported as such rather than papered over.

data.detail:

Containment requirement held throughout: orchestrator finalizes all edges/summary nodes itself and reports only success/failure + bare counts.

WP progression: WP-0 spike gated the design; WP-1 shipped the three agents; WP-2 rewrote the skill; WP-3 added the docstring demarcation line; WP-4 verified in-repo (including kill-mid-run resume) but could NOT exercise the degraded/no-nesting fallback path — 2/2 scratch-plugin attempts failed to even connect the scratch MCP server, never reaching the intended "cognition tools present, Agent absent" condition; accepted as untested-with-safe-failure-mode (anti-fabrication guard behaved correctly under real total-tool-absence: honest "could not run, re-run to resume", no fabrication) and filed as follow-up task 673bd85885df — left OPEN as the epic's one remaining child, low priority, nesting GA since v2.1.172.

WP-5 (human install gate) PASSED — Colton ran the hands-on 2026-07-03 post-pin: /plugin update pulled 0.15.0 into cache, restart surfaced all three curate-* agents as launchable types, and the first INSTALLED-cache orchestrator run completed 364s fully autonomous with a bare-counts-only report ("1 uncurated node processed → 2 edges created, 0 discarded, 0 clusters → 0 summary nodes"). Ground-truthed on the journal: both edges source=curate-skill verbatim, correct led_to direction (the two WP-4 degraded-mode discoveries → the follow-up task), mark_curated fired after commit, two child-agent spawns (edge-analyzer + cluster-analyzer) real. The WP-4 MCP flake did not reappear installed. data.summary: vibe-cognition plugin release procedure
data.detail:
Procedure to cut a vibe-cognition plugin release. This repo ships CODE ONLY and does NOT carry its own marketplace.json (a second file named "coltondyck" would collide — Claude Code keys marketplaces by name). The marketplace lives in the separate repo Haagndaazer/colton-claude-plugins (marketplace name "coltondyck"), maintained by Loki.

Steps:
1. Make your code changes on a WP branch.
2. If the change is user-facing, bump the version in BOTH pyproject.toml AND .claude-plugin/plugin.json (the plugin system reads version from plugin.json), and run `uv lock` so uv.lock self-version matches.
3. Pass the WP gate: sonnet-reviewed spec, manager isolated-worktree verify (ruff + whole-repo pyright + pytest at the pinned SHA), CI green on all 3 legs (ubuntu 3.11/3.13, windows 3.11).
4. Merge to main. This is the "code commit" — the SHA the marketplace will pin.
5. Flush the live journal to main via a temp worktree (clean-prefix verified).
6. Ping Loki with the code-commit SHA and the version. Loki re-pins that SHA in colton-claude-plugins's marketplace.json and pushes, so installs/updates pick it up.
7. CONFIRM with Loki that local==remote on the marketplace repo after the re-pin, and that plugin.json + pyproject.toml both read the new version at the pinned SHA.
8. Release gates on a human's machine: install-mechanics fixes can't be self-verified (no headless plugin install/update harness). Have a human run /plugin update and confirm the new version loads. The hands-on checklist MUST cover, whenever the release touches these areas:
   a. The empty/unset CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR case — verify .cognition/ lands in the PROJECT directory (not the plugin install root) and that a deliberately-empty REPO_PATH produces a clear validation error.
   b. If the release ships or touches plugin-defined agents: after the real marketplace-pinned /plugin update, confirm the new/changed agent types are actually launchable (they surface in the agent picker post-restart) — a version bump alone does not prove the agent definitions loaded.
   c. If the release touches a background-agent pipeline (e.g. curate-orchestrator): confirm a real run launches from the INSTALLED cache (not just an isolated dev venv) and completes end-to-end with the pipeline's intended report shape.
   d. If the release changes anything model-pin-related: re-check that model pins are honored in the INSTALLED context specifically — dev-venv or isolated evidence alone does not prove the installed plugin resolves the same pins; if the installed-run transcript can't be read directly, say so as an explicit caveat rather than treating isolated evidence as equivalent.

The marketplace sha always points to the code commit on this repo's main (a journal-flush-only commit on top has 0 code delta and is fine to pin too, but convention is to pin the code commit).
context: v3 of the release workflow — folds in the WP-Curate-Background WP-5 human install-gate checklist (agent-launchability
model-pin installed re-check) as standing step 8 sub-items so future releases touching these areas inherit the check instead of re-deriving it per-WP. Supersedes b721512f812c (v2).

data.summary: Installed-context model-pin failure: curate-cluster-analyzer spawned as SONNET instead of its haiku frontmatter pin (observed by Colton on a v0.15.0 production run) — contradicts WP-0's pin-honored finding
data.detail: Colton observed the curate-cluster-analyzer subagent running on Sonnet instead of Haiku during a v0.15.0 installed-cache curation run (2026-07-03). This is the exact cost-bug + standing-rule violation the WP-0 spike checked for; WP-0's isolated --plugin-dir test (discovery d79cd9a93a02) had concluded frontmatter pins ARE honored on nested Agent() calls with no explicit model override, and the orchestrator prompt was therefore written to deliberately NOT pass a model override on its analyzer spawns. Either the installed-cache context behaves differently from --plugin-dir, the nested-spawn pin behavior differs per agent/position, or the WP-0 finding was wrong/overgeneralized. The WP-5 gate note had honestly recorded that installed-context pins were NOT directly re-verified (empty transcript files) — this observation closes that gap with a failure. Impact: cost only (Sonnet doing a Haiku fan-out job), no correctness/containment issue — the run's output and containment were verified good. Fix direction (belt-and-suspenders): curate-orchestrator.md should pass an EXPLICIT model: "haiku" on both analyzer Agent() calls — an explicit override is authoritative regardless of frontmatter-pin behavior, and is harmless if the pin also works. Root-cause investigation should still determine why the pin didn't hold installed (and re-test the orchestrator's own sonnet pin at top-level launch).
data.author: Vince

data.summary: v0.15.1: fix analyzer model pins — explicit model:"haiku" on the orchestrator's Agent() spawns + root-cause why installed frontmatter pin didn't hold
data.detail: Colton observed curate-cluster-analyzer running SONNET on a v0.15.0 installed run (fail f09e770da046), contradicting WP-0's pin-honored finding (d79cd9a93a02, --plugin-dir context). Fix (belt-and-suspenders, ship regardless of root cause): edit agents/curate-orchestrator.md Steps 2+3 — REMOVE the "do not pass an explicit model override" instruction and its d79cd9a93a02 citation, REPLACE with: always pass model: "haiku" explicitly on both curate-edge-analyzer and curate-cluster-analyzer spawns (explicit override is authoritative; harmless if the frontmatter pin also works). Root-cause in parallel: reproduce with Vorpid's raw-JSONL method against the INSTALLED cache — compare edge-analyzer vs cluster-analyzer spawn model IDs in one run, check whether the orchestrator's own top-level sonnet pin held, and characterize --plugin-dir vs installed-cache difference; record findings (if pins are simply unreliable for plugin agents in nested spawns, that supersedes d79cd9a93a02's generalization). Release as 0.15.1 through the standard chain: suite+ruff, code commit, Vince journal flush + Loki ping, runbook v3 step-8 checklist on Colton's machine (its installed model-pin re-check is what caught this — verify it passes this time with direct evidence, not inference).

data.summary: Root-cause repro attempt for f09e770da046 partially confirms the pin, doesn't explain the failure: edge-analyzer's resolvedModel=haiku ground-truthed via nested Agent tool_result metadata, but cluster-analyzer's equivalent metadata was simply absent and both children's own transcript files were empty
data.detail:
Attempted Vorpid's raw-JSONL method against the INSTALLED cache, reusing the curate-orchestrator background run launched right after the WP-Curate-Background closing episode (agentId ab84b932295995c65, 2 uncurated nodes, 5 edges, 0 clusters — the same run reported to Vince as curation-complete).

Read the orchestrator's own JSONL transcript directly (not self-report). Found:
- The `curate-edge-analyzer` spawn's Agent tool_result carried a structured `toolUseResult` dict with `resolvedModel: "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"`, `agentId`, and `outputFile` fields — direct, non-self-reported confirmation the haiku pin held for THIS spawn on the installed cache.
- The `curate-cluster-analyzer` spawn's Agent tool_result had NO such structured metadata at all — its tool_result was plain text (the standard "Async agent launched..." boilerplate) with no `resolvedModel` field anywhere in the transcript (grepped: exactly 1 occurrence of the string "resolvedModel" in the whole file, the edge-analyzer's). Both children's own output transcript files (`a2923ceba277ad041.output`, `a92737a5a110d14f7.output`) remained 0 bytes even after the parent run completed — the same "installed-run transcript files came up empty" limitation the WP-5 gate note flagged.

Net result: could confirm the pin held for edge-analyzer in this repro (consistent with d79cd9a93a02's --plugin-dir finding, now also true for one installed-cache nested spawn), but could NOT get any direct evidence — for or against — on what model cluster-analyzer actually ran on in this specific run, because the metadata exposure itself was inconsistent between the two calls and the fallback (reading the child's own transcript) was unavailable. This neither reproduces nor refutes Colton's f09e770da046 observation (Sonnet on cluster-analyzer in a separate real v0.15.0 production run) — it identifies a distinct, real gap in the verification method itself: `resolvedModel` metadata on a nested Agent tool_result is not reliably populated call-to-call, so it cannot be treated as a dependable ground-truth channel by itself. Does NOT supersede d79cd9a93a02 (that finding's specific claim, edge-analyzer / no-override, is reconfirmed here) — it's a narrower finding about tooling visibility, filed alongside f09e770da046 rather than retracting it.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: Model routing verified with direct resolvedModel evidence: background agents DO receive explicit model params (haiku/sonnet under a Fable main), nested children DO downgrade below their parent when explicit; frontmatter pins held top-level and for edge-analyzer nested, but resolvedModel metadata is inconsistently populated
data.detail: Live verification (2026-07-03, Colton's request after fail f09e770da046) via per-agent resolvedModel fields read from raw session transcripts (~/.claude/projects/.../*.jsonl + subagents/*.jsonl). Findings: (1) BACKGROUND + EXPLICIT PARAM HONORED — a background agent launched with model:"haiku" from a claude-fable-5 main session resolved to claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 (1.5s trivial run); another with model:"sonnet" resolved to claude-sonnet-5. (2) NO PARAM = INHERIT — a background agent launched with no model param resolved to claude-fable-5 (inherited the main instance's model; cost hazard on big-model sessions). (3) NESTED DOWNGRADE HONORED — a sonnet background parent spawning a child with explicit model:"haiku" produced a child transcript with resolvedModel claude-haiku-4-5-20251001; independently, Vorpid's installed-cache repro showed the orchestrator's nested edge-analyzer at claude-haiku-4-5 (discovery 86f5d959b78c). (4) FRONTMATTER PIN HELD TOP-LEVEL — both installed-cache curate-orchestrator background launches (no model param) resolved to claude-sonnet-5 per the frontmatter pin, NOT the main session's fable-5. (5) THE OBSERVED FAILURE (f09e770da046: cluster-analyzer on Sonnet) remains unreproduced-but-credible: in Vorpid's repro the cluster-analyzer's tool_result carried NO resolvedModel field at all — the metadata is inconsistently populated call-to-call, so pin behavior cannot be monitored reliably without explicit overrides. NET GUIDANCE: always pass an explicit model param on Agent calls where cost matters (both directions — prevents inheriting a bigger main model AND pin flakiness); frontmatter pins are passive defense only; resolvedModel in parent transcripts is the ground-truth read but may be absent.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: MCP loading hygiene: make vibe-cognition server connection reliable at session start (currently fragile/random, user often must /mcp reconnect)
data.detail: Colton (2026-07-04): connecting the vibe-cognition MCP server at session start is fairly fragile and random — it often fails to connect on a new session and needs a manual /mcp reconnect. Investigate the startup path end-to-end (plugin.json mcpServers launch via uv run --directory, venv health, uv sync timing in session-start.sh, model/chromadb init blocking the MCP handshake, Windows process pileup from WP-Server-Lifecycle) and harden so first connect succeeds every time. Related prior evidence: MCP cold-start flakiness in --plugin-dir spike runs (discovery c98a5f3b6d7f), scratch-plugin server failed to connect same way (discovery 07f231aa00eb). Acceptance: repeated fresh-session starts (N>=10) all connect first try on the primary dev machine; failure mode, if any remains, is diagnosable from a log the user can read.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: MCP/tool-surface legibility: make workflow + document tools discoverable from PUSHED surfaces
data.detail: Epic. Agents consistently forget to use cognition_get_workflow (retrieval) and the document tools (store/descriptor-node linking) because every trigger that would prompt reaching for them lives in the tools' own docstrings — a PULL surface an agent only reads AFTER it already decided to call the tool (proven live: cognition tools sit behind deferred loading, so their descriptions aren't in context until selected by name). Fix moves the triggers into the pushed layer (SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS + prime digest) and repairs the garbled record docstring + soft skill descriptions. Four work packages: DW1 (SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS rewrite), DW2 (prime Workflows section + doc count), DW3 (cognition_record NODE TYPES repair + get_workflow-local retry wording), DW4 (skill-description MUST-framing). Vorpid implements; Vince gates diffs at pinned SHA + release via Loki. Rejected: new dedicated tools/renames (consumer churn, speculative) — revisit only if surface fix doesn't move usage. Acceptance: every changed tool string usable from name/args/description/return-shape alone (workflow 67751ebc39bd audit); pushed surfaces name workflows+documents as first-class recordables AND retrievables.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Root cause of "agents forget workflow/document tools": discovery triggers live in the PULL layer (tool docstrings), never the PUSHED layer
data.detail: MCP-surface audit (2026-07-04). The tool docstrings are individually good; the defect is layering. Findings: F1 — SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS practice 2 enumerates recordables (decisions, failures, discoveries, constraints, patterns, episodes) but NEVER workflow or document; workflow RETRIEVAL is only a tacked-on final "Also:" paragraph; documents appear nowhere in pushed text. F2 — the session-start prime digest surfaces tasks/constraints/patterns/decisions/incidents but NO workflows or documents, so an agent has zero evidence a workflow exists and nothing triggers get_workflow (the graph has a standing tool-surface-audit workflow right now, unmentioned at session start). F3 — triggers live in the wrong layer: tool docstrings are a PULL surface read only after the agent already decided to call the tool; proven live this session — cognition tools sit behind deferred/ToolSearch loading, so their descriptions aren't in context until selected by name. F4 — cognition_record NODE TYPES docstring is garbled (workflow missing from the type list; ENTITY NODES header has empty body running straight into WORKFLOW NODES, so entity "summary MAX 250 chars" guidance visually attaches to workflows, contradicting their verbose rule). F5 — no pushed write-side triggers ("new runbook -> workflow node"; "handed spec/PDF -> store_document + descriptor nodes"); they exist only in SKILL.md frontmatter and vibe-workflow's description is SOFT ("Use this skill to...") with no retrieval trigger vs vibe-document's "You MUST...". Fix = move triggers to pushed surfaces (SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS + prime), repair the docstring, harden the skill descriptions.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Fix MCP-surface legibility by moving triggers to pushed surfaces, NOT by adding dedicated tools or renaming
data.detail: Decision (MCP-surface fix, 2026-07-04). CHOSEN: rewrite the pushed layer — add workflow+document to SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS practice 2's record list and promote workflow/task gates from the "Also:" afterthought to a first-class practice; add a prime-digest "## Workflows" section (HEAD titles) + document count so agents see procedures every session and post-compact; repair the cognition_record NODE TYPES docstring; give vibe-workflow's skill description MUST-framing symmetric with vibe-document. REJECTED: (a) new dedicated tools e.g. cognition_record_workflow — imposes consumer churn on every existing caller for speculative benefit; (b) tool renames for name-level affordance — same churn. Rationale: the tools work; the problem is that nothing in the PUSHED (always-in-context) layer tells agents they exist or when to reach for them, so fixing the surface text is the minimal high-leverage change. Revisit dedicated tools ONLY if the surface fix fails to move measured usage. Highest-leverage single item = the prime "## Workflows" section (DW2): it puts real procedure names in front of the agent every session and after every compact.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: WP-DW1: rewrite SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS — workflows+documents as first-class record + retrieval triggers
data.detail: File: src/vibe_cognition/instructions.py (SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS constant). (1) Practice 2 (RECORD AS YOU WORK) record-list must add: workflow nodes (for reusable multi-step procedures/runbooks) and documents (via cognition_store_document + descriptor nodes citing the doc_ref in references). (2) Replace the tacked-on final "Also:" paragraph with a FIRST-CLASS practice covering: cognition_list_tasks before picking up work; cognition_get_workflow before ANY multi-step task; store a handed spec/PDF/doc via cognition_store_document + descriptor nodes; record a newly-worked-out runbook as a workflow node. Constraints: ASCII-only (Windows stdout safety — see module docstring); token ceiling ~450; RE-MEASURE (char-count/4) and UPDATE the cost comment at lines ~34-41 (currently says ~370). Supersedes the insufficient WP-7 fix (does NOT close done-task 0da12a816d7f). Acceptance: pushed text names workflow AND document as both recordable and retrievable; passes the tool-surface self-sufficiency audit (workflow 67751ebc39bd). Pairs with DW2 for shared token accounting.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: WP-DW2: prime digest "## Workflows" section (HEAD titles) + stored-document count line
data.detail: Files: src/vibe_cognition/cognition/prime.py + src/vibe_cognition/config.py. Add a "## Workflows" section to the prime digest listing stored workflow HEAD titles — supersession-resolved (get_nodes_by_type("workflow") then drop nodes that have an incoming SUPERSEDES edge; cheap in-memory O(n), NO embeddings needed). Cap by a NEW config field prime_workflow_limit (default ~5); truncate with the existing "+N more — use cognition_get_workflow" overflow idiom (pin the convention, no silent cap). Add a one-line stored-documents count (get_nodes_by_type("document") length). CRITICAL: the new prime_workflow_limit config field MUST be plumbed into BOTH PrimeConfig construction sites — prime.py main() (~lines 290-299) AND instructions.py main() (~lines 107-115) — or the post-compact reinject path silently drifts from the startup path. Acceptance: fresh session AND post-compact both show workflow HEAD titles; env-var override works in both paths; overflow line present when > limit. Highest-leverage item in the epic. Pairs with DW1.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: WP-DW3: repair cognition_record NODE TYPES docstring + get_workflow-local embeddings-retry wording
data.detail: File: src/vibe_cognition/tools/cognition_tools.py. (1) cognition_record NODE TYPES section (~lines 1662-1689): add `workflow` to the enumerated type list WITH a write trigger ("a reusable multi-step procedure/runbook — record when you work one out"); fix the ENTITY NODES/WORKFLOW NODES garble (the ENTITY NODES header currently has an empty body running straight into WORKFLOW NODES, so entity "summary MAX 250 chars" guidance visually attaches to workflows and contradicts their verbose-detail rule). NOTE workflow nodes are created via cognition_record(node_type="workflow"); confirm the docstring says so. (2) In cognition_get_workflow (~line 2355): wrap the embeddings-loading error LOCALLY with wording that says "embeddings still loading — retry in a few seconds; this does NOT mean no workflow exists." Do NOT edit shared require_embeddings (utils.py:19-32) — cognition_search shares it and the generic text must not leak workflow-specific wording. (3) Run the tool-surface self-sufficiency audit (workflow 67751ebc39bd) over every changed string. Acceptance: NODE TYPES lists workflow with trigger + no garble; get_workflow loading error distinguishes "retry" from "none exist"; shared error text untouched. Pairs with DW4.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: WP-DW4: vibe-workflow SKILL.md MUST-framing + retrieval trigger, symmetric with vibe-document
data.detail: Files: skills/vibe-workflow/SKILL.md (+ verify skills/vibe-cognition/SKILL.md). Rewrite the vibe-workflow frontmatter `description` from the current SOFT "Use this skill to store..." to MUST-framing symmetric with vibe-document's ("You MUST use this skill whenever..."). It MUST carry BOTH sides of the trigger: the WRITE trigger (you worked out a reusable multi-step procedure -> record it as a workflow node) AND the RETRIEVAL trigger (before starting any multi-step task, check for an existing workflow first via cognition_get_workflow). The retrieval trigger currently lives only in the skill body — hoist it into the description (the pulled-at-selection surface). Then verify skills/vibe-cognition/SKILL.md routes/points to BOTH sub-skills (vibe-workflow and vibe-document). Acceptance: vibe-workflow description is MUST-framed and states write+retrieval triggers; vibe-cognition SKILL routes to both. Pairs with DW3.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: WP-7's SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS fix (done-task 0da12a816d7f, commit ec48490) was necessary but INSUFFICIENT — it produced the inadequate "Also:" afterthought
data.detail: While auditing the MCP surface (2026-07-04) found that closed task 0da12a816d7f ("SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS: add tasks-first / workflow-first gates"), shipped in WP-7 (commit ec48490), is exactly the tacked-on final "Also:" paragraph in SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS. It technically added the workflow/task gates to the pushed contract, so the task was correctly marked done — but as a bolted-on afterthought AFTER the curate paragraph, not woven into practice 2's record-list, and it added workflow RETRIEVAL only (never workflow/document as recordables, never documents at all). Net: agents still forget the tools. LESSON: adding a gate to the pushed text is necessary but not sufficient; placement + first-class integration into the enumerated practices is what makes it land. WP-DW1 supersedes (not reopens) this — do NOT reopen 0da12a816d7f; the correct record is this discovery + the DW1 rewrite. Pattern candidate for curation: "closing a legibility task on presence-of-text rather than integration-quality leaves the defect live."
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Closing a legibility/discoverability task on presence-of-text, not integration-quality, leaves the defect live
data.detail: WP-7 (task 0da12a816d7f, shipped commit ec48490) added workflow/task pre-work gates to SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS and was correctly marked done — the text existed. But it landed as a bolted-on final "Also:" paragraph after the main practices, never woven into the enumerated record/retrieval practices, and covered workflow retrieval only (never documents, never workflow/document as recordables). Net effect: agents kept forgetting the tools: the underlying defect (discovery 30d2ccb3f0b0 — triggers live in the PULL layer, never surfaced to agents before they've already decided to call a tool) was untouched. Lesson: when closing a task whose goal is "agents notice and use X," verify integration/placement quality (is it a first-class, front-loaded practice an agent will actually read before deciding?), not just that the string was added somewhere in the pushed contract. Presence-of-text is necessary but not sufficient.
data.author: curate-orchestrator

data.summary: Prime-digest sections that count/list a SUPERSEDES-versioned node type must HEAD-filter (exclude incoming-SUPERSEDES nodes) — _format_document_count missed this, inflating the count
data.detail: Caught at the DW2 gate by the adversarial review layer (2026-07-04), verified by Vince. In Package 1 (branch wp-dw1-dw2-pushed-layer @ 6a249b0), prime.py _format_workflows correctly excludes superseded old versions (nodes with an incoming SUPERSEDES edge, matching get_workflow_head's HEAD definition), but its sibling _format_document_count raw-counts len(get_nodes_by_type(DOCUMENT)) with NO such filter. Documents genuinely version via supersession: _validate_supersedes_shape (cognition_tools.py:325) legalizes SUPERSEDES when from/to are the SAME node type ("newer version of the same thing"), which covers document->document; the prior_version_id re-store path surfaces exactly this. So a document revised N times reports N+1 "stored documents" in the digest. Display-only over-count (no crash/data loss), but inconsistent with the feature's own sibling convention and misleads the awareness signal the line exists to provide. REUSABLE LESSON: any prime section (or similar surface) that counts or lists a node type which can carry SUPERSEDES edges must apply the same "no incoming SUPERSEDES" HEAD filter as _format_workflows — and needs a supersession-exclusion test mirroring test_generate_prime_superseded_workflow_excluded. The test-coverage asymmetry (workflow had the exclusion test, document did not) is what let it through.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: v0.15.2 shipped: MCP/tool-surface legibility epic 0d3172f5bd32 — workflows+documents moved into the pushed layer, NODE TYPES/get_workflow docstrings repaired, skill MUST-framing
data.detail:
Full narrative (2026-07-04). PROBLEM: agents consistently forgot cognition_get_workflow (retrieval) and the document tools because every trigger lived in the PULL layer (tool docstrings, invisible until a tool is already selected — proven live: cognition tools sit behind deferred/ToolSearch loading). ROOT CAUSE recorded in discovery 30d2ccb3f0b0; approach decision 79851871289c (fix pushed surfaces, reject new tools/renames); WP-7's prior fix found necessary-but-insufficient (discovery 81fd774ad2d9, re done-task 0da12a816d7f — the tacked-on "Also:" paragraph). DECOMPOSITION: epic 0d3172f5bd32 with four WP children, Vorpid implementing, Vince gating.

PACKAGE 1 (DW1 0eeb49fbbcc7 + DW2 1a2f187a8280, branch wp-dw1-dw2-pushed-layer): SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS rewritten to a four-practice contract naming workflows+documents as recordable AND retrievable (ASCII-only, 433 tokens, cost comment updated); prime digest gained a "## Workflows" HEAD-resolved section (supersession-filtered via get_predecessors(SUPERSEDES), matching get_workflow_head) + a stored-document count line, capped by new config prime_workflow_limit wired into BOTH PrimeConfig sites. PACKAGE 2 (DW3 84de49cb4e37 + DW4 4a85f4630d7b, branch wp-dw3-dw4-pull-layer): cognition_record NODE TYPES garble fixed + workflow added with a write trigger; cognition_get_workflow embeddings-loading error wrapped LOCALLY to distinguish "retry" from "no workflow exists" (shared require_embeddings untouched, so cognition_search keeps generic text); vibe-workflow SKILL description given MUST-framing symmetric with vibe-document; vibe-cognition SKILL confirmed already routing to both.

GATE: Vince ran own read + adversarial subagent + own test runs at pinned SHAs + tautology checks (revert-and-rerun) + composition (both merged into a throwaway integ branch, combined suite 631 pass). The adversarial layer CAUGHT one real defect Vince's own read missed: _format_document_count raw-counted documents without the supersession HEAD-filter its sibling _format_workflows applies (documents version via same-type SUPERSEDES) — held Package 1, Vorpid fixed in one line + mirror test (discovery dfd9f59827b1), re-gated tautology-clean. RELEASE: version 0.15.2 (Colton chose patch over 0.16.0); Vorpid built release-0.15.2 (merged both tips + bumped pyproject.toml + plugin.json), Vince verified scope/version/ff-ability + ran suite (631 pass), fast-forwarded main to c5f4a3c, pinged Loki to re-pin the marketplace. Vorpid For-the-record: SUPERSEDES direction confirmed empirically (newer->older); no failures/surprises; a mid-run "worktree reverted" system note was a false alarm from Vince's tautology-check revert in a sibling worktree sharing relative paths (not data loss). Full gate discipline held; nothing shipped on a handed green.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Plan: MCP first-connect reliability + concurrency-safe launch hardening (task ced8f0401bd2, v0.15.3)
data.detail:
# Investigation + Hardening Plan — MCP first-connect reliability (task ced8f0401bd2)

Owner (implementer): Vorpid · Gate: Vince · Priority: high
Status: APPROVED — ship autonomously (Colton 2026-07-04). Peer-reviewed (Sonnet, conditional-go, corrections folded).

## MANDATE (Colton 2026-07-04)
Do everything you can to make this more stable to launch. Ship it autonomously. Ensure current use ability remains as-is. Concurrent agents still need to be able to use it at the same time without any issues or collisions — this is a PRIMARY FEATURE of the plugin. We can revert or fix after shipping+testing. Colton won't upgrade other currently running agents until Vince confirms the latest works as intended.

Implications:
- PRIMARY HARD GATE #1 — concurrency safety. Multiple agents each run their OWN MCP server against the SAME shared venv (${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/.venv), the SAME shared ChromaDB, and the SAME shared journal. Nothing here may introduce a collision, lock, or corruption across concurrent server processes. Must be demonstrated with a cross-process concurrency test, not argued.
- PRIMARY HARD GATE #2 — zero regression. Existing capability stays exactly as-is; full suite stays green; existing concurrent behavior preserved.
- Autonomous ship: WP-A and WP-B ship TOGETHER this release. Colton's controlled rollout (holds his running fleet on the prior version until confirmation) replaces the human-reproduction gate. Vince still hard-gates the diff (concurrency + regression) before release.

## Root-cause hypotheses (H1/H2 co-primary; H4 closed prior-art; H5 added)
- H1 (co-primary): server-launch `uv run` (no --no-sync in plugin.json) re-syncs/locks the shared venv concurrently with the hook's uv-run calls -> Windows lock contention -> handshake stalls past connect timeout. Matches recorded venv-contention hypothesis 17a9148fb887.
- H2 (co-primary, promoted): synchronous ChromaDB open in lifespan blocks the handshake. mcp SDK runs the lifespan async-context to its yiel
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Ship MCP launch-stability hardening (v0.15.3) autonomously, with concurrency-safety + zero-regression as PRIMARY gates; H1/H2 co-primary; --no-sync only with a read-only guard
data.detail: Decision (2026-07-04, Colton-approved autonomous ship). Two co-primary root causes of the fragile MCP first-connect: H1 the server launch lacks --no-sync so it re-syncs/locks the shared venv concurrently with the SessionStart hook; H2 the lifespan opens ChromaDB synchronously before the MCP handshake yield (blocking by construction; lands on the open rust-backend flake e09d4f4a9a23). CHOSEN plan (two packages, one release): WP-A = bounded-retry around the ChromaDB open + concurrency-safe startup breadcrumbs (self-verifiable, strictly risk-reducing); WP-B = add --no-sync to the plugin.json server launch ONLY paired with a READ-ONLY pre-import venv-ready guard in server.py main() that fast-fails-with-message on a broken venv and NEVER runs uv sync itself (the hook owns healing; N concurrent servers self-syncing would collide). PRIMARY CONSTRAINTS from Colton: (1) concurrent multi-agent use is a primary feature — no collisions/locks/corruption across concurrent servers on the shared venv+ChromaDB+journal, proven by a cross-process concurrency test not argued; (2) zero regression to existing capability. REJECTED: bare --no-sync without the guard (would convert intermittent failure into deterministic first-install failure); server self-healing via uv sync (concurrency collision). Human-reproduction pre-gate REPLACED by Colton's post-ship controlled rollout (he holds his running fleet on the prior version until Vince confirms; revert is the fallback). H4 (git subprocess hang) is closed prior-art (fixed v0.12.1). Full plan in document doc:30c6fd11c6a8.
data.author: Vince

transitions.note: Approved for autonomous ship (v0.15.3). Plan doc:30c6fd11c6a8. Two packages WP-A/WP-B filed as children; concurrency-safety + zero-regression are primary gates.

data.summary: WP-A: ChromaDB open bounded-retry + concurrency-safe startup breadcrumbs (self-verifiable, risk-reducing)
data.detail: Ship-now half of ced8f0401bd2. (1a) Wrap the two synchronous ChromaDB calls in server.py lifespan's pre-yield path (embeddings/storage.py:46-80 — get_collection, get_or_create_collection) in a bounded retry so a transient rust-backend InternalError (open flake e09d4f4a9a23, MORE likely under concurrent opens) doesn't fail the MCP handshake. Self-verify: simulate the InternalError, confirm retry absorbs it. (1b) Add startup breadcrumbs (server start -> uv resolve done -> lifespan enter -> ChromaDB open start/done -> handshake yield -> bg thread/model-loaded); hook stamps its three uv-run start/end times so hook-vs-server venv overlap is visible. HEISENBUG GUARD (mandatory): NO synchronous flushed disk writes in the pre-yield critical section; capture in-memory time.monotonic() stamps, defer the file write to after-yield or a background thread; pre-yield trail via the stderr Claude Code already captures. CONCURRENCY-SAFE: the breadcrumb log must be per-PID or append-safe — never a single shared file N servers collide on. Acceptance: retry test passes (simulated InternalError absorbed); breadcrumbs present + concurrency-safe; full suite green; no pre-yield sync disk write. Plan: doc:30c6fd11c6a8.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: WP-B: --no-sync on the plugin.json server launch + READ-ONLY concurrency-safe pre-import guard in server.py
data.detail: The launch-contention half of ced8f0401bd2. Add `--no-sync` to the server launch args in .claude-plugin/plugin.json so the spawn stops re-syncing/locking the shared venv (kills H1 contention + H5 cold-start tax; strictly better for N concurrent launches). MANDATORY PRECONDITION — do NOT ship bare --no-sync: the SessionStart hook's sync is NOT guaranteed to finish before Claude Code spawns the server (600s hook timeout for ~8-min first-install syncs implies they run concurrently; no serialization guarantee). Bare --no-sync would make the server launch against an absent/partial venv and fail FAST + DETERMINISTICALLY on first-install/post-update. So pair it with a READ-ONLY pre-import guard in server.py main() (~line 502): try importing the heavy natives (torch, chromadb); if broken/missing, emit a CLEAR failure message and exit. CONCURRENCY-SAFE: do NOT have the server run `uv sync` itself — the hook owns healing (600s budget); N concurrent servers + the hook all syncing the same venv would collide. Healthy venv (steady-state + every existing install) -> import passes -> proceed fast, never touch the venv lock. Acceptance: guard proven NOT to fire on a healthy venv (existing installs unaffected — zero regression); guard fast-fails with a clear message on a broken/absent venv; server never runs uv sync; CROSS-PROCESS concurrency test (multiple servers/opens on the same venv+ChromaDB+journal, no collision/lock/corruption — extend c34c788b8d5b intent) passes; full suite green. Connect-reliability effect confirmed by Colton's post-ship controlled rollout (revert is fallback). Plan: doc:30c6fd11c6a8.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: torch/sentence_transformers loads at MODULE IMPORT (generator.py:9), in the pre-handshake critical path on EVERY server launch — a likely cold-start latency contributor separate from the backgrounded model-weights load
data.detail: Found while grounding the WP-B guard review (2026-07-04). No `import torch` exists anywhere in src/vibe_cognition/ — torch is pulled in transitively. But generator.py:9 does `from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer` at MODULE TOP LEVEL, and importing sentence_transformers imports torch. Import chain: server.py:13 `from .embeddings import EmbeddingGenerator` -> embeddings/__init__ -> generator.py:9 -> sentence_transformers -> torch. So the torch LIBRARY import is paid when `python -m vibe_cognition.server` loads its modules, BEFORE main()/lifespan/handshake. Only the model WEIGHTS load (SentenceTransformer(model_name) at generator.py:62, in __init__) is deferred to the background thread. CONSEQUENCE: (1) the WP-B _venv_ready guard importing torch is FREE (already cached at module load) — no latency regression, concern refuted. (2) The torch import (multi-second cold on Windows, Defender scanning torch DLLs/.pyd) sits in the pre-handshake critical path every launch — a plausible contributor to the fragile/slow first-connect (H5-adjacent). The WP-A breadcrumbs (module-load -> module-loaded delta) will quantify it. CANDIDATE OPTIMIZATION (separate, regression-risky, NOT in the WP-A/WP-B floor): make sentence_transformers a LAZY import — move generator.py:9 into the backend __init__/from_config where SentenceTransformer is actually used (line 62), so torch loads in the background thread instead of the pre-handshake module-import path. Philosophically identical to the already-accepted "background the model load" pattern. Verify nothing else imports SentenceTransformer at module scope + TYPE_CHECKING hints intact.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Floor (WP-A+B @cfc19f0) gated PASS; GO on WP-C lazy-import to ship with the floor as v0.15.3 — BUT it must reconcile with the WP-B guard, which imports torch at module load and would otherwise neutralize the win
data.detail: Gate outcome + scope decision (2026-07-04). FLOOR (WP-A retry+breadcrumbs, WP-B --no-sync+guard) at cfc19f0 PASSES Vince's own gate: full suite 651 pass/1 skip; the cross-process concurrency test (6 real OS subprocesses, same persist_dir, convergence asserted) ran 3/3 clean for me (~17s each); retry catches ONLY InternalError and is bounded (~0.15s); the two "modified" test files are pure additions (no existing test edited); _venv_guard is read-only (never uv sync) and correctly placed BEFORE the heavy .embeddings import; retry tests are tautology-clean (ImportError against reverted storage.py). I independently measured server module import at 12.48s (Vorpid: 15.25s) — CONFIRMS torch/sentence_transformers at module import (generator.py:9) is the dominant pre-handshake cost, which the floor's --no-sync/retry do NOT address. GO on WP-C: lazy sentence_transformers import to move that load off the pre-handshake module-import critical path into the existing background thread; ships WITH the floor as v0.15.3 (Colton's "do everything to make launch stable" + autonomous-ship mandate). Its core benefit is SELF-VERIFIABLE (before/after `python -c "import vibe_cognition.server"` time). CRITICAL INTERACTION: the WP-B _venv_guard imports torch at module load (REQUIRED_MODULES chromadb+torch) — so moving generator.py:9 alone is neutralized; the guard still pays torch. Resolution: guard should REAL-import chromadb (needed pre-yield in lifespan anyway) but use importlib.util.find_spec for torch (presence check, no load); a broken-DLL torch then fails in the background thread and hits the EXISTING embedding_error graceful-degradation path (get_status shows error, tools degrade) instead of crashing the handshake — strictly better. Vorpid measures the torch/ST/chromadb breakdown first, reconciles the guard, proves import-time before/after + regression + concurrency. Also: v0.15.3 release must run `uv lock` after the bump (uv.lock self-version drifted 0.15.1->0.15.2 unbumped in the 0.15.2 release; open task 76eb74437d89).
data.author: Vince

data.summary: WP-C: lazy sentence_transformers import to move the ~12-15s torch load off the pre-handshake critical path (reconcile with the WP-B guard)
data.detail: Ships with WP-A/WP-B as v0.15.3. Confirmed dominant cost: server module import is 12-15s (Vince 12.48s, Vorpid 15.25s), almost entirely torch/sentence_transformers loading at generator.py:9 (module top), in the pre-handshake critical path every launch. MOVE it off: make `from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer` lazy — import inside the backend __init__/from_config where it's used (generator.py:62), so it loads in the EXISTING background thread (_load_embeddings_and_sync), not at server module import. CRITICAL INTERACTION (do not miss): _venv_guard.py (WP-B) imports torch at module load via REQUIRED_MODULES=("chromadb","torch") — moving generator.py:9 alone is NEUTRALIZED because the guard still pays the torch import pre-handshake. Reconcile: guard should REAL-import chromadb (it's needed pre-yield in lifespan regardless) but use importlib.util.find_spec("torch") (presence-only, no load) — a broken-DLL torch then fails in the background thread and hits the EXISTING embedding_error graceful-degradation path (embedding_ready still set, get_status shows error) rather than crashing the handshake. STEPS: (1) instrument/measure the torch vs sentence_transformers vs chromadb share of the 12-15s first (breadcrumbs between imports) so we know the real win. (2) lazy generator.py import; verify nothing else imports SentenceTransformer at module scope; TYPE_CHECKING hints intact. (3) reconcile the guard (chromadb import + torch find_spec). PROOF (self-verifiable): before/after `python -c "import vibe_cognition.server"` time — expect a large drop (target sub-second-ish module import, torch/ST moved to background). REGRESSION: full suite green; broken-torch degrades gracefully (background) not a handshake crash — add/keep a test. CONCURRENCY: cross-process test still green; each process imports ST once in its own background thread (no shared-state race). Plan doc:30c6fd11c6a8, decision (this gate).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: v0.15.3 RC (5028770) adversarial gate: mechanism verified sound on both hard gates; two cheap hardening fixes folded before ship (startup-timing temp-file retention, retry-exhaustion log). PersistentClient race is a THIRD manifestation of e09d4f4a9a23
data.detail: Final adversarial review of the WP-A+B+C release candidate (5028770), Vince-gated 2026-07-04. VERIFIED SOUND: (1) lazy sentence_transformers import — grep confirmed no surviving module-scope reference to the SentenceTransformer symbol anywhere but generator.py; the changed test mock (patch sentence_transformers.SentenceTransformer) correctly intercepts the now-call-time lazy import; single construction site per process + CPython import lock = no thread race. (2) Guard find_spec gap is by design and degrades gracefully: a broken-DLL torch (passes find_spec, fails real import) is caught in _load_embeddings_and_sync's except (server.py:397-403) which sets embedding_error, STILL sets embedding_ready (no hang), and get_status surfaces "error: ..."; pinned by new test test_broken_embedding_backend_degrades_gracefully. My own runs: full suite 655x2 (both orders, no error), concurrency test clean under full-suite load + 3x isolated, import 12.48s->4.83s, tautology-clean (5 WP-C tests fail vs reverted floor). TWO HARDENING FIXES folded before ship (not blockers, but cheap + on-mandate): (a) _startup_timing writes %TEMP%/vibe-cognition-startup/pid-<PID>.log per server process with NO retention/cleanup — a NEW unbounded leak in a GLOBAL temp dir across every session of every project; add bounded, concurrency-safe, best-effort prune (by age / keep-recent-N; never delete a file younger than the threshold so a concurrent server's fresh file survives). (b) _retry_chromadb_open re-raises a bare InternalError on exhaustion — add a WARNING log naming attempts so a production failure is diagnosable ("exhausted N retries") per the task's own acceptance. Left retry attempts at 3 (no evidence 3 is insufficient; the log makes exhaustion diagnosable so we can widen data-driven from Colton's rollout). ACCEPTED as-is: the module-level breadcrumbs list is appended cross-thread without a lock — benign under the GIL (list.append atomic), diagnostic-only. DURABLE FACT (Vorpid For-the-record): chromadb.PersistentClient() construction itself races under concurrent first-open ("table collections already exists", SharedSystemClient) — a THIRD manifestation of the e09d4f4a9a23 InternalError class (after get_collection, get_or_create_collection), all absorbed by the same _retry_chromadb_open primitive.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: v0.15.3 shipped: MCP launch-stability epic ced8f0401bd2 — lazy torch import (12-15s->~5s module load off the pre-handshake path), ChromaDB open bounded retry, --no-sync + read-only venv guard, startup breadcrumbs + bounded retention
data.detail:
Full narrative (2026-07-04). PROBLEM: vibe-cognition MCP server connected fragilely/randomly at session start (Colton often had to /mcp reconnect). MANDATE: make launch stable, ship autonomously, PRESERVE existing capability, and keep concurrent multi-agent use collision-free (a primary feature); Colton holds his running fleet on the prior version until Vince confirms; revert is the fallback. Plan: doc:30c6fd11c6a8; approach decision 9022f7de94e9; gate decision 3687a82e6e54.

ROOT CAUSE (the investigation's payoff): the dominant first-connect cost was ~12-15s of torch/sentence_transformers loading at MODULE IMPORT (generator.py:9 top-level import), in the MCP handshake's pre-yield critical path on every launch (discovery da7857ef13cc; the mcp SDK runs lifespan to its yield before handling initialize). The --no-sync/retry floor did NOT address it; the lazy-import (WP-C) did.

DELIVERED (WP-A 7a74b5e41e30, WP-B d4c2c84c87f6, WP-C 502f0099fd41, one branch): (A) _retry_chromadb_open — bounded (3 attempts, ~0.15s), InternalError-only, wraps ALL THREE chromadb open points; PersistentClient() construction itself was found to race under concurrent first-open ("table collections already exists", SharedSystemClient) — a THIRD manifestation of the pre-existing flake e09d4f4a9a23, all absorbed by the one primitive. (B) plugin.json --no-sync + a read-only _venv_guard.py imported before the heavy .embeddings import (a main()-placed guard would be dead code — the module-top import crashes first on a broken venv); guard reconciled with WP-C to real-import chromadb but presence-check torch via find_spec, so a broken-DLL torch degrades via the existing embedding_error path rather than crashing. (C) lazy sentence_transformers import moved into the backend __init__ (background thread) — measured module import 12.48s->4.83s. Plus concurrency-safe per-PID startup breadcrumbs with mtime-based bounded retention (prune), and a retry-exhaustion warning log.

GATE (Vince, hard on the two primary requirements): own reads + adversarial subagent (verified lazy-import correctness + graceful degradation directly; found 2 cheap hardening gaps — temp-file leak, retry-exhaustion diagnosability — folded before ship) + own test runs (full suite 661 pass across multiple runs) + a REAL cross-process concurrency convergence test (6 OS subprocesses, same persist_dir; passed under full-suite load AND isolated) + tautology checks (reverted-source failures) + import-time measurement. Zero existing tests weakened (one test's mock PATH changed, coupled to the lazy import, assertion identical). RELEASE: v0.15.3, Vorpid built release-0.15.3 (merge + bump pyproject.toml/plugin.json + `uv lock` which also closed the stale-lock drift 76eb74437d89); Vince verified scope/versions/ff + ran suite, fast-forwarded main to 581c7c3, pushed, pinged Loki. TRANSPARENCY: the pre-existing e09d4f4a9a23 chromadb flake surfaced twice under full-suite load in an UNRELATED test (hnsw segment reader) — confirmed not introduced/worsened by this epic; cross-process concurrency clean in isolation; priority bumped as follow-up. NEXT: Colton runs the controlled rollout test before upgrading his fleet.
data.author: Vince

transitions.note: Approved for autonomous ship (v0.15.3). Plan doc:30c6fd11c6a8. Two packages WP-A/WP-B filed as children; concurrency-safety + zero-regression are primary gates.
Shipped in v0.15.3 (main @ 581c7c3). Root cause was the 12-15s torch module-import; lazy import cut module load to ~5s. Episode 8fbf310765d5. Colton to run controlled rollout test.

WP-B shipped in v0.15.3 @ 581c7c3 (--no-sync + read-only _venv_guard, reconciled with WP-C to find_spec torch). Episode 8fbf310765d5.
